Title: Senior Data Scientist
Description:
Samba is a media intelligence company. We know what the world is watching, reading, and thinking about — in real time, at scale, across every screen. Our data exists with the consent of over a billion people, organized into the most complete picture of consumer attention ever built. The biggest brands in the world use that picture to make smarter decisions. We think it’s the most interesting data asset on the planet, because it’s the most culturally relevant. 







We are hiring a Senior Data Scientist in Amsterdam to help shape Samba TV's data science function for the agentic era of advertising. You will define modeling methodology, build production ML systems, and apply modern AI and agentic capabilities across our data products.




You bring 8+ years of experience, deep expertise in machine learning and modern AI, and the technical range to take methodology from research through production deployment. You are an active collaborator with engineering, product, and partner teams, and an advocate for rigorous, defensible modeling practice.







What You'll Do
Modeling and ML Development






Own end-to-end delivery of data science projects, from problem scoping through production deployment.








Define and ship modeling methodology that powers Samba's data products, including model selection, evaluation frameworks, and reproducibility standards.








Apply solid command of core ML and statistics (regression, classification, clustering, model evaluation, experimental design, causal inference) to billion-row, real-world data.








Build production-quality Python and PySpark on Databricks: well-tested, documented, reusable.








Partner with Data Engineering to define data requirements, validate pipelines, and ensure model inputs are reliable and production-ready.






AI and Agentic Capabilities






Build and operate advanced AI systems using modern methodologies: retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), LLM-augmented modeling and Graph Neural Networks. Design AI-driven modeling approaches that improve as signals evolve, supporting agentic decision-making at platform scale.








Integrate LLMs and agentic workflows into production ML pipelines where they extend modeling capability and unlock new product surfaces.






Technical Contribution and Collaboration






Drive technical design for modeling components within your scope, producing clear solution documents covering problem statement, approach, metrics, and trade-offs.








Translate business requirements into modeling solutions in close collaboration with product, engineering, and go-to-market partners.








Uphold high standards for production-quality data science.








Mentor data scientists on the team through structured feedback, pairing, and design review.






MLOps and Production Practice






Establish and operate MLOps practices: experiment tracking, pipeline orchestration (Airflow), model monitoring, retraining workflows, and reproducibility standards.








Apply privacy-compliant data handling practices, including GDPR, CCPA, and Samba's data governance policies.











Who You Are
Required






8+ years of hands-on data science experience with a Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Data Science, Computer Science, Mathematics, or a related quantitative field (or 6+ years with a Master's, 3+ years with a PhD, or equivalent).








Demonstrated ability to own and deliver complex, multi-sprint data science projects from problem scoping through production deployment.








Solid command of core ML and statistics, including neural networks, regression, classification, clustering, model evaluation, experimental design, and causal inference, applied to billion-row datasets.








Track record of building methodology, not just applying it: data analysis, model selection, evaluation frameworks, and solid documentation of decision processes








Production experience with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus, pgvector, or equivalent) for retrieval, matching, or inference at scale.








Advanced Python with production-quality, tested code; strong SQL and PySpark on billion-row datasets.








Databricks, Delta Lake, and job orchestration (Airflow); hands-on production experience on AWS, GCP, and Databricks.








MLOps proficiency: experiment tracking, pipeline orchestration, model monitoring, reproducible deployment.








Experience designing and operating agentic AI systems in production: prompt engineering, agent orchestration, tool use, or integration of LLMs into ML pipelines.








A clear communicator who translates technical work into design docs, user stories, and cross-functional conversations.








An active mentor who invests in others, gives direct feedback, and raises the bar for the team as a whole.






Preferred






Knowledge graph design (RDF, OWL, SPARQL, or equivalent graph frameworks), Natural Language Processing, Background in ad tech, CTV/OTT, ACR, audience activation, identity resolution, or measurement methodologies.








Experience with causal inference (A/B testing, synthetic control, uplift modeling).














Base salary is just one component of Samba total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include bonuses, short-term incentives, and long-term incentives. In addition, Samba provides health insurance, wellness offerings, life and disability insurance, a retirement savings plan, paid holidays and paid time off (PTO), and other employee benefits.




Samba is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.  We strive to empower connection with one another, reflect the communities we serve, and tackle meaningful projects that make a real impact.




 




Samba may collect personal information directly from you, as a job applicant, Samba may also receive personal information from third parties, for example, in connection with a background, employment or reference check, in accordance with the applicable law. For further details, please see Samba's Applicant Privacy Policy. For residents of the EU , Samba Inc. is the data controller.




We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Scientist - QuantumBlack, AI by McKinsey
Description:
Who You'll Work With

Driving lasting impact and building long-term capabilities with our clients is not easy work. You are the kind of person who thrives in a high performance/high reward culture - doing hard things, picking yourself up when you stumble, and having the resilience to try another way forward.

In return for your drive, determination, and curiosity, we'll provide the resources, mentorship, and opportunities you need to become a stronger leader faster than you ever thought possible. Your colleagues—at all levels—will invest deeply in your development, just as much as they invest in delivering exceptional results for clients. Every day, you'll receive apprenticeship, coaching, and exposure that will accelerate your growth in ways you won’t find anywhere else.

When you join us, you will have:


Continuous learning: Our learning and apprenticeship culture, backed by structured programs, is all about helping you grow while creating an environment where feedback is clear, actionable, and focused on your development. The real magic happens when you take the input from others to heart and embrace the fast-paced learning experience, owning your journey.
A voice that matters: From day one, we value your ideas and contributions. You’ll make a tangible impact by offering innovative ideas and practical solutions, all while upholding our unwavering commitment to ethics and integrity. We not only encourage diverse perspectives, but they are critical in driving us toward the best possible outcomes.
Global community: With colleagues across 65+ countries and over 100 different nationalities, our firm’s diversity fuels creativity and helps us come up with the best solutions for our clients. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to learn from exceptional colleagues with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
World-class benefits: On top of a competitive salary (based on your location, experience, and skills), we provide a comprehensive benefits package to enable holistic well-being for you and your family.


Your Impact

As a Data Scientist, you will collaborate with clients and interdisciplinary teams to develop impactful analytics solutions, optimize code, and solve real-world business problems across diverse industries. You’ll grow as a technologist by contributing to cutting-edge projects, R&D, and global conferences while working alongside world-class talent in a dynamic, innovative environment.

In this role, you will partner with clients to understand their needs and develop impactful analytics solutions. You will translate business problems into analytical challenges, build models to solve them, and ensure they are evaluated with relevant metrics. You’ll also contribute to internal tools, participate in R&D projects, and have opportunities to attend and present at conferences like NIPS and ICML.

Your work will create real-world impact. By identifying patterns in data and delivering innovative solutions, you will help clients maintain their competitive advantage and transform their day-to-day operations. Your contributions will directly influence business outcomes and drive lasting improvements across industries.

You will be in Amsterdam or Brussels, and collaborate closely with data scientists, data engineers, machine learning engineers, designers, and product managers around the world working on interdisciplinary projects that use math, statistics, and machine learning to derive insights from raw data. You will help global companies transform their businesses and enhance performance across industries such as healthcare, automotive, energy, and elite sports.

At McKinsey, you’ll thrive in an unparalleled environment for growth. You’ll develop a sought-after perspective by connecting technology and business value, tackle real-life challenges across diverse industries, and collaborate with inspiring multidisciplinary teams, gaining a holistic understanding of AI and its potential to drive transformation.

Our Tech Stack

While we advocate for using the right tech for the right task, we often leverage the following technologies: Python, PySpark, the PyData stack, SQL, Airflow, Databricks, our own open-source data pipelining framework called Kedro, Dask/RAPIDS, container technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes, cloud solutions such as AWS, GCP, and Azure, and more.

Your Qualifications and Skills


Bachelors, Masters or PhD level in a discipline such as: computer science, machine learning, applied statistics, mathematics, engineering or artificial intelligence.
0-2+ years of professional experience in applying machine learning and data mining techniques to real problems with copious amounts of data
Programming experience (focus on machine learning): R and/or Python (must), SPSS, SAS, Ruby, Hadoop (valued).
Ability to prototype statistical analysis and modeling algorithms and apply these algorithms for data driven solutions to problems in new domains
Ability to independently own and drive model development, balancing demands and deadlines.
Demonstrated aptitude for analytics.
While we advocate for using the right tech for the right task, we often leverage the following technologies: Python, PySpark, the PyData stack, SQL, Airflow, Databricks, our own open-source data pipelining framework called Kedro, Dask/RAPIDS, container technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes, cloud solutions such as AWS, GCP, and Azure, and more.
Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, in English, with the ability to adjust your style to suit different perspectives and seniority levels. Knowledge of French or Dutch is valued.
Exceptional time management to meet your responsibilities in a complex and largely autonomous work environment.
Good presentation and communication skills, with the ability to explain complex analytical concepts to people from other fields.
Willingness to travel.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Company Description

Wilderway (WW) is working to deliver investable nature restoration, by developing landscape ecosystem restoration projects aligned with rewilding principles, financed through the sale of high quality, high integrity carbon and nature credits. Our pipeline is strong, with projects at various stages of development - from feasibility studies, land contracting and carbon and nature credit development, right through to contracting multiple (corporate) credit buyers. We work with experienced local partners who are specialised in delivering nature restoration on the ground.




We are looking for a passionate and dedicated Data Scientist.




Role

The Data Scientist will sit at the core of Wilderway’s technical work and will focus on:

supporting the development and maintenance of data pipelines and analytical tools,
assisting with the implementation and improvement of (carbon and nature) models across existing and new projects,
analysing and integrating monitoring and operational datasets,
supporting the management and improvement of WW’s data systems and infrastructure.




The Data Scientist will report to the Impact and MRV Lead and will work in close collaboration with the Carbon & Nature Data Scientist, Impact and MRV manager as well as the credit developers in WW team and with local teams responsible for project implementation in the field.




Main Responsibilities




The main responsibilities of the Data Scientist are:

Data Analysis and Model Support

Contribute to the development and maintenance of models for different standards and habitats, in collaboration with Impact and MRV team members.
Operation of models for specific projects, adapting them to the changing circumstances.
Assisting in updating the models after each monitoring cycle, based on new data.

Data Management

Contributing and implementing WW’s data management solutions.
Manage, clean, structure, and validate project datasets.
Contribute to building and maintaining data pipelines and databases.
Support the development of dashboards and data visualisation tools.

Monitoring

Support in analysing monitoring and operational datasets, ensuring data quality and

consistency.

Collaborate with team members to integrate field and external datasets into WW systems.




Knowledge and Experience

Passionate about Wilderway’s mission, vision and objectives.
Dedicated to make a difference for nature restoration, with a commercial mindset.
Master's degree or above in a relevant discipline (e.g., Environmental Engineering, Data Science).
1-3 years of working experience in developing and implementing models and data management.
Experience working with large and complex datasets, including data cleaning, transformation, and validation.
Proven work/study expertise in advanced use of GIS, remote sensing, R & python, and in (ecological) modelling systems.
Experience in using version control systems (e.g. Git/GitHub) for collaborative development and code management.
Good communication skills, to develop user guidance and explaining concepts to non-technical audience.
Proactive and willing to learn in a fast-paced and mission-driven environment.
Interest in applying data science to real-world problems.




Requirements

• Location: based in The Netherlands or Spain (in proximity to Burgos).

• Hybrid workplace (2-3 working days per week from the office), with travel flexibility across Europe as required.

• Permit to work in Europe required.




Remuneration

Wilderway offers competitive compensation packages, aligned with experience, qualifications, and the

role’s responsibilities. We are a dynamic, purpose-driven organization with an ambitious agenda and a

commitment to both personal and professional growth for our employees.




Application

We invite all those interested in this position to submit a motivational letter and CV before 5pm on

30th June 2026 to info@wilderway.com.

Please include Data Scientist in the subject line.

All queries regarding this position should also be sent to this email address.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer – Cloud | MLOps | GenAI (Relocation provided)
Description:
Are you a Machine Learning Engineer with a passion for building real-world, production-grade AI solutions in the cloud? 

At Wypoon Technologies, we’re expanding our network of skilled ML Engineers to support leading clients across the Netherlands. From predictive analytics to real-time recommendations and Generative AI use cases,you’ll work on meaningful projects that scale. 

Whether your focus lies in model development, MLOps, or cloud-native architecture, we’re looking for engineers who can bring machine learning to life in enterprise environments. 




Why Join Us? 




At Wypoon Technologies, you’ll work on dynamic projects for industry leaders such as Heineken, ING, ASML, Philips, and Shell, tackling exciting challenges and delivering tailored enterprise solutions. 




Here’s what makes us stand out: 

✔ Impactful Work: Shape the future of our clients’ digital landscapes. 

✔ Innovative Environment: Collaborate within a supportive team that values learning and growth. 

✔ State-of-the-art technology: Access the latest tools and platforms. 

✔ Global Opportunities: Dreaming of living and working in the Netherlands or Belgium? We provide full support with visa processes, making your transition smooth and hassle-free! 

  




Key Responsibilities
Designing and implementing machine learning pipelines in cloud environments (Azure, AWS, or GCP) 
Developing and deploying models for classification, regression, time series, recommendation, or NLP use cases 
Working with structured and unstructured data, and applying feature engineering, model tuning, and evaluation techniques 
Packaging and deploying models using containerization (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) 
Automating and monitoring ML workflows using MLflow, Airflow, or cloud-native tools (SageMaker, Vertex AI, Azure ML) 
Collaborating with data scientists, engineers, and product teams to translate business problems into ML solutions 
Contributing to MLOps practices: model versioning, CI/CD for ML, performance monitoring, and rollback strategies 




Requirements
At least 5 years of professional experience. 
Advanced proficiency in English (minimum B2 level in speaking, writing, listening, and reading). 
A bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field. 
Strong proficiency in Python, including libraries such as scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, or XGBoost 
Experience building and deploying ML models in at least one major cloud platform: Azure, AWS, or GCP 
Familiarity with ML pipeline orchestration and CI/CD practices 
Experience with Generative AI use cases, such as working with LLMs, embedding models, prompt engineering, or custom GPT integrations 
Familiarity with HuggingFace Transformers, LangChain, or RAG architectures 
Exposure to Responsible AI, explainability, or ethical ML practices 
Background in MLOps tooling: MLflow, DVC, Tecton, Feast 
Experience with data labeling tools or ML observability platforms 
Solid understanding of software engineering principles and cloud infrastructure 
Experience working with APIs, data lakes, and distributed systems is a plus 
Comfortable in Agile environments and cross-functional teams 





What We Offer
Competitive Compensation: Salary tailored to your skills and experience. 
Generous Time Off: 25 paid vacation days (based on a 40-hour work week). 
Job Security: A direct and permanent contract from day one! 
Relocation Support: A relocation and travel budget to ease your transition. 
Tax Benefits: Eligibility for the 30% ruling. 
Travel Reimbursement: We've got your daily commute covered. 
Top-Notch Equipment: A high-quality device to ensure you work efficiently. 
Professional Development: Financial support for certifications, technical training, and language courses—because we value your long-term career growth. 
Family: Visa sponsorship for your partner and children under 18. 
Temporary Accommodation: Stay in temporary housing while we help you find your permanent home. 
Comprehensive Relocation Assistance: Extensive support to help you settle in smoothly. 
Community & Fun: Enjoy team events and connect with developers from around the globe! 







Join Wypoon Technologies, where your expertise will thrive, your ideas will be valued, and your career will reach new heights. 

Apply now to be part of our transformative journey!




We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Why Join Us? 


At Wypoon Technologies, you’ll work on dynamic projects for industry leaders such as Heineken, ING, ASML, Philips, and Shell, tackling exciting challenges and delivering tailored enterprise solutions. 


Here’s what makes us stand out: 

✔ Impactful Work: Shape the future of our clients’ digital landscapes. 

✔ Innovative Environment: Collaborate within a supportive team that values learning and growth. 

✔ State-of-the-art technology: Access the latest tools and platforms. 

✔ Global Opportunities: Dreaming of living and working in the Netherlands or Belgium? We provide full support with visa processes, making your transition smooth and hassle-free! 

  


Key Responsibilities
  • Designing and implementing machine learning pipelines in cloud environments (Azure, AWS, or GCP) 
  • Developing and deploying models for classification, regression, time series, recommendation, or NLP use cases 
  • Working with structured and unstructured data, and applying feature engineering, model tuning, and evaluation techniques 
  • Packaging and deploying models using containerization (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) 
  • Automating and monitoring ML workflows using MLflow, Airflow, or cloud-native tools (SageMaker, Vertex AI, Azure ML) 
  • Collaborating with data scientists, engineers, and product teams to translate business problems into ML solutions 
  • Contributing to MLOps practices: model versioning, CI/CD for ML, performance monitoring, and rollback strategies 


Requirements
  • At least 5 years of professional experience. 
  • Advanced proficiency in English (minimum B2 level in speaking, writing, listening, and reading). 
  • A bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field. 
  • Strong proficiency in Python, including libraries such as scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, or XGBoost 
  • Experience building and deploying ML models in at least one major cloud platform: Azure, AWS, or GCP 
  • Familiarity with ML pipeline orchestration and CI/CD practices 
  • Experience with Generative AI use cases, such as working with LLMs, embedding models, prompt engineering, or custom GPT integrations 
  • Familiarity with HuggingFace Transformers, LangChain, or RAG architectures 
  • Exposure to Responsible AI, explainability, or ethical ML practices 
  • Background in MLOps tooling: MLflow, DVC, Tecton, Feast 
  • Experience with data labeling tools or ML observability platforms 
  • Solid understanding of software engineering principles and cloud infrastructure 
  • Experience working with APIs, data lakes, and distributed systems is a plus 
  • Comfortable in Agile environments and cross-functional teams 


What We Offer
  • Competitive Compensation: Salary tailored to your skills and experience. 
  • Generous Time Off: 25 paid vacation days (based on a 40-hour work week). 
  • Job Security: A direct and permanent contract from day one! 
  • Relocation Support: A relocation and travel budget to ease your transition. 
  • Tax Benefits: Eligibility for the 30% ruling. 
  • Travel Reimbursement: We've got your daily commute covered. 
  • Top-Notch Equipment: A high-quality device to ensure you work efficiently. 
  • Professional Development: Financial support for certifications, technical training, and language courses—because we value your long-term career growth. 
  • Family: Visa sponsorship for your partner and children under 18. 
  • Temporary Accommodation: Stay in temporary housing while we help you find your permanent home. 
  • Comprehensive Relocation Assistance: Extensive support to help you settle in smoothly. 
  • Community & Fun: Enjoy team events and connect with developers from around the globe! 



Join Wypoon Technologies, where your expertise will thrive, your ideas will be valued, and your career will reach new heights. 

Apply now to be part of our transformative journey!

These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Build the systems behind the intelligence




At RebelsAI, we turn the promise of Generative AI into products that work. We help organizations build real capabilities with AI, not just tools, but systems that scale, adapt and create impact. Our machine learning engineers design and build the technical backbone of these solutions, from data preprocessing to secure cloud deployment.




We are looking for someone who knows how to bring AI into the real world. Someone who understands the tech, sees the patterns and wants to build the next generation of intelligent infrastructure.




What you will do

As a machine learning engineer, you build the core components that power our GenAI solutions. You work on everything from ingestion to inference, across scalable cloud environments. Together with our consultants and clients, you turn business needs into smart, usable and production-ready systems.




You will

Work closely with AI Experts, Implementation Leads and clients to solve real problems
Set up infrastructure using containers, APIs and serverless components
Preprocess, chunk and structure unstructured data for optimal use in prompts and context injection
Design, build and deploy AI-based applications in the Microsoft Azure cloud
Configure and manage vector databases such as Pinecone or Azure AI Search
Implement retrieval-augmented generation flows using vector databases and embedding models
Develop orchestration logic for multi-step agents and tool-using language models
Monitor system performance, latency, accuracy and cost
Apply data privacy and security best practices across all layers
Explore new GenAI frameworks and contribute to our engineering standards
Adopt AI-tools such as Cursor and MCP's to enhance your productivity




What you bring

You have a strong technical foundation and a curious mindset. You are eager to build real things and see them used in the world.
A degree in AI, Data Science, Computer Science or a related field
Strong Python skills and experience/interest in building end-to-end applications
A good understanding of machine learning principles and data analysis
Familiarity with LLM frameworks such as LangChain
Understanding of embedding models and how vector search fits into retrieval-based systems
Working knowledge of cloud environments such as Azure, GCP or AWS
Fluent in Dutch




The kind of Rebel we are looking for

You want to build things that work. You enjoy solving technical challenges, experimenting with new tools and creating clean, reliable systems that others can use and scale. You take ownership, move fast with focus and are not afraid to ask the questions that matter.




You are

Curious about how AI works and how to make it useful
Confident working independently and collaboratively
Practical and driven to get things live
Open to feedback and eager to grow
Passionate about technology and how it can help people move forward




What we offer

A fast-growing team where your work shapes real AI products
Room to experiment and work with the latest GenAI tools and techniques
A license for an AI coding tool of your choice, like Cursor or GitHub Copilot
Competitive salary
Laptop and travel expense reimbursement
Hybrid working with a home base in Rotterdam
Flexibility to work 4 or 5 days per week
A smart, kind and ambitious team that learns and builds together




Ready to build what is next

If this sounds like the kind of challenge you are looking for, we would love to meet. Send us your resume and a short message about why this role fits you. Want to learn more about us? Visit rebelsai.nl or check our updates on LinkedIn. We welcome all backgrounds and perspectives.




‼️We know LinkedIn sometimes shows big numbers, but please don’t let that discourage you. We’re hiring for multiple roles and personally review every single application. Including yours.‼️

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist – Search & Generative AI







About the Organization

Our client is a leading financial organization with a strong digital focus, where innovation, data, and technology play a central role. Millions of users rely on its digital platforms, applications, and online services every day. To continuously enhance the user experience, the organization invests heavily in intelligent search capabilities and AI-driven solutions.




As part of an ongoing digital transformation, the organization is building a next-generation search architecture that combines semantic search, embeddings, machine learning, and generative AI technologies.




About the Environment

You will join a multidisciplinary Agile team consisting of experienced software developers, a Scrum Master, and a Product Owner. The team has a strong technical foundation and works collaboratively to develop innovative search and AI solutions.




The department is focused on delivering an exceptional digital experience through advanced content platforms, intuitive navigation, and intelligent search capabilities. The solutions you develop will be used across websites, applications, and internal platforms that serve large numbers of users on a daily basis. The environment encourages innovation, knowledge sharing, and experimentation while operating within well-defined governance and compliance frameworks.




Responsibilities.

As a Data Scientist, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and optimizing intelligent search and AI solutions. You will translate complex business challenges into scalable data products and contribute to the evolution of a future-proof search ecosystem. Your responsibilities will include:




Developing and training machine learning models for semantic search and personalization.
Optimizing embedding models and hybrid search solutions within Elasticsearch environments.
Researching and implementing Large Language Model (LLM) use cases, including summarization, metadata generation, and other AI-driven applications.
Translating business requirements into actionable data products and insights.
Designing dashboards and visualizations to communicate insights effectively.
Collaborating closely with developers, the Product Owner, and Scrum Master to deliver scalable solutions.
Monitoring, evaluating, and improving model performance, quality, and explainability.
Supporting AI governance, compliance initiatives, and risk management processes.
Coaching team members in data-driven ways of working and helping establish robust data practices.




Requirements

We are looking for a Data Scientist who combines strong technical expertise with the ability to engage stakeholders and communicate complex concepts effectively. Required Qualifications:

Minimum of 3 years of experience in Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing (NLP)
Proven expertise in information retrieval, semantic search, embeddings, and Large Language Model integration.
Experience with Elasticsearch and hybrid search architectures.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
Experience with search evaluation methodologies and metrics such as nDCG, MAP, and MRR.
Advanced Python programming skills.
Experience working in notebook environments, such as Databricks.
Ability to balance innovation with governance, compliance, and risk management requirements.




Preferred Qualifications

Experience with machine learning model monitoring and explainability.
Knowledge of CI/CD practices and software development best practices.
Experience with dashboarding, data visualization, and storytelling.
Familiarity with Agile/Scrum methodologies and multidisciplinary teams.
Basic knowledge of Java is considered a plus.




What You Will Receive

An opportunity to work in an innovative, data-driven environment with significant business impact.
Exposure to cutting-edge technologies in Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Search, and Generative AI.
Collaboration with highly skilled professionals in multidisciplinary Agile teams.
A culture that encourages continuous learning, innovation, and professional development.
The opportunity to contribute to large-scale digital products used by millions of users.
Competitive compensation and benefits aligned with your experience and expertise.
A key role in shaping the future of intelligent search and AI-driven digital experiences.

Title: Data Scientist - Data & Business
Description:
Senior Data Scientist




📍Eindhoven, NL

💼 Full-time, hybrid

🌍 EU citizenship required

🗣️ Native-level Dutch essential




This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys working with data but also likes being close to the business. You’d be joining a data-driven environment where customer, behavioural, and commercial data are used to help well-known brands make smarter decisions.

It’s a role with a good mix of modelling, stakeholder contact, and strategic input!







You’ll be:

Translating business questions into clear data solutions and recommendations
Building, implementing, and maintaining data science models
Working on attribution models, predictive models, and clustering
Combining and analysing data from different sources using SQL, NoSQL, and cloud-based platforms
Collaborating with specialists across data, analytics, engineering, and commercial teams
Presenting insights in a way that is clear, practical, and useful for stakeholders







You’ll bring:

A university background in Data Science, Econometrics, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or something similar
3+ years of experience in a hands-on Data Science role
Strong Python skills and experience with SQL and/or NoSQL databases
A solid understanding of statistics and machine learning
Confidence in speaking with stakeholders and turning technical work into business value
Strong communication skills in both Dutch and English




Bonus points for:

Experience with Marketing Mix Modelling
Experience with attribution modelling or marketing analytics
A strong interest in using data to drive commercial growth







Some of the perks and benefits:

Flexible working hours and hybrid setup
Option to work abroad within the EU for a period of time
Plenty of room for growth and development
Training opportunities in both technical and soft skills
Travel reimbursement
26 holiday days plus the option to buy more
...and more.




If you’re a Data Scientist who likes combining hands-on modelling with real business impact, this could be a really interesting one.

Title: AI Engineer
Description:
In één oogopslag

Locatie: Amersfoort, Deventer, Zwolle of Venlo als standplaats en daarnaast eventueel bij klanten door heel Nederland.
Werkervaring: 3+ jaar
Aantal uren: 32-40 uren per week




Dit ga je doen

Als AI Engineer bij Fellowmind ben je voornamelijk bezig met het ontwerpen, bouwen en doorontwikkelen van AI-oplossingen voor onze klanten. Je werkt nauw samen met de andere Business Lines binnen Fellowmind waarbij je opereert op het snijvlak van AI en innovatie; als zij er niet meer uitkomen ben jij degene die ze raadplegen. Je hebt dan ook ervaring met het toepassen van 'best practices' en bent niet bang om nieuwe AI-technologiën en –innovaties te verkennen.




Met jouw scherpe vragen achterhaal je niet alleen de behoefte van onze klanten, maar jij vertaalt deze ook naar een passende, veilige en verantwoorde oplossing. Hierbij ben je een waardevolle bron van kennis en expertise voor je collega's. Op het gebied van AI help je hen de vertaalslag te maken van theorie naar praktijk. Het leuke hieraan is dat wij opereren in veel verschillende focusmarkten, waaronder manufacturing, finance en healthcare.




Je zult deel uitmaken van de nieuwe Business Line AI, een Business Line die is gespecialiseerd in het ontwikkelen van low- en pro-code AI-toepassingen op het Microsoft platform. Op dit moment zijn dat vooral Gen. AI agents, maar we zijn ook een propositie aan het ontwikkelen rond Machine Learning.




Een dag als AI Engineer

De werkdag begint! Je start rustig met een kop koffie en opent je projectdashboard. Om 09:00 uur is het tijd voor de dagstart met collega’s en de klant. Tijdens deze meeting bespreek je het dagprogramma en identificeer je acties die extra aandacht nodig hebben of waarbij hulp gewenst is.




Vervolgens ga je gefocust aan de slag met het ontwerpen en bouwen van een nieuwe AI-oplossing voor een klant in de financiële sector. Met de Semantic Kernel stel je een multi-agent workflow op die complexe vraagstukken opsplitst. Eén agent haalt data op uit Azure Dataverse, een tweede genereert visualisaties van die data en een derde produceert inzichten op basis van reasoningmodellen via Azure OpenAI. Je voegt je testdataset toe en gebruikt Promptflow om de agents te evalueren. Daarna deploy je twee varianten om A/B-tests uit te voeren. Via Application Insights krijg je inzicht in de prompts en monitor je promptdrift.




Na een productieve ochtend is het tijd voor de lunch. Dit is een ideaal moment voor een wandeling, een potje pingpong of om in onze Social Clubroom samen met collega’s te eten.

In de middag heb je een meeting met een klant uit de supply chain-sector. Samen met een Technology Specialist, een aantal Functioneel Consultants en een BI Consultant brainstorm je over manieren om backorders te optimaliseren met ATP in de keten. Met gerichte vragen ontdek je de kern van hun uitdagingen en vertaal je hun behoeften naar een passende AI-oplossing.




Om de dag af te ronden, bereid je je voor op de AI Development Day van morgen. Samen met Max (AI Business Line Expert) en Reuben & Lucas (AI Engineers) bedenk je welke nieuwe producten en diensten we kunnen ontwikkelen om onze klanten nog beter te bedienen.




Je kijkt er extra naar uit, want morgenmiddag ga je werken aan de doorontwikkeling van een bestaande AI-agent voor een klant in de manufacturing-sector. Daar zal je hun OpenAPI-specificatie exporteren en ombouwen in een MCP-server die de prestaties van de agent verbetert. Daarbij durf je nieuwe technologieën te verkennen en toe te passen.




Dit bieden wij jou

Onze mensen staan aan de basis van ons succes! Daarom doen we er alles aan om ervoor te zorgen dat iedereen het naar zijn zin heeft. Geen strakke hiërarchie, collega’s die elkaar graag helpen, ruimte en tijd voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling en kennisdeling: dat is bij Fellowmind vanzelfsprekend!




Natuurlijk bieden we jou ook een mooi pakket arbeidsvoorwaarden. Iedereen is anders en ook de wensen ten aanzien van arbeidsvoorwaarden lopen uiteen. Daarom is een deel van ons pakket flexibel en kun je bepaalde zaken inrichten naar je eigen wens.




Wat je in ieder geval kunt verwachten:

Een goed salaris, passend bij de functie die je gaat vervullen en de kennis en ervaring die je meeneemt.
Een aantrekkelijke bonusregeling gebaseerd op onze company performance.
28 vakantiedagen op basis van een fulltime dienstverband. Je hebt de mogelijkheid om vakantiedagen bij te kopen of te verkopen van een apart flexbudget.
De mogelijkheid om 2x per jaar een lief & leed dag op te nemen waarin je de ruimte krijgt voor vrijwilligerswerk, mantelzorg of, bijvoorbeeld, een verhuizing.
Een gezonde balans tussen werken vanaf kantoor, op locatie bij onze klanten en thuis.
Bewegen is gezond! Maak een keuze uit onze aantrekkelijke sportieve regelingen zoals het leasen van een fiets of een bijdrage aan jouw sport abonnement.
Een inspirerende werkplek in Venlo, Deventer, Zwolle of Amersfoort waar je vkunt genieten van goede koffie en thee, fruit, soep en fris. Je bent van alle gemakken voorzien!
Reizen is een vast onderdeel van ons werk. Van huis naar klant, kantoor of naar een externe locatie. Voor iedere functie bieden we een passende regeling voor mobiliteit (lease of vergoeding).
We helpen je bij jouw unieke, persoonlijke ontwikkeling.
We geloven in focus en aandacht en daarom hebben we onze ‘focustijd’ ingericht. Tijdens de focustijd krijg je de ruimte om dingen te kunnen afronden, te werken aan jouw ontwikkeling of een kennissessie te bezoeken. Door slim te plannen zorgen we zo voor focus gedurende jouw werkdag.




Wie ben jij?

Voor het ontwikkelen van onze low- en pro-code AI-oplossingen gebruiken we uiteenlopende tools. Staar je echter niet blind op het lijstje hieronder: het is slechts een overzicht van kennis en ervaring waarmee je een vliegende start kunt maken:




Je hebt 3+ jaar ervaring als Data Scientist, Data Engineer, Machine Learning Engineer óf Software Engineer;
Je hebt ervaring met programmeren in C# en/of Python en gerelateerde (Gen.) AI libraries en frameworks (bv. Pyspark, Tensorflow/Keras, pytorch, SKlearn, Langchain, Autogen);
Je hebt affiniteit met het Microsoft platform (bv. Copilot Studio / Power Virtual Agents, Azure AI);
Je beheerst de Nederlandse- en Engelse taal in woord en geschrift;

Een dag als AI Engineer

De werkdag begint! Je start rustig met een kop koffie en opent je projectdashboard. Om 09:00 uur is het tijd voor de dagstart met collega’s en de klant. Tijdens deze meeting bespreek je het dagprogramma en identificeer je acties die extra aandacht nodig hebben of waarbij hulp gewenst is.


Vervolgens ga je gefocust aan de slag met het ontwerpen en bouwen van een nieuwe AI-oplossing voor een klant in de financiële sector. Met de Semantic Kernel stel je een multi-agent workflow op die complexe vraagstukken opsplitst. Eén agent haalt data op uit Azure Dataverse, een tweede genereert visualisaties van die data en een derde produceert inzichten op basis van reasoningmodellen via Azure OpenAI. Je voegt je testdataset toe en gebruikt Promptflow om de agents te evalueren. Daarna deploy je twee varianten om A/B-tests uit te voeren. Via Application Insights krijg je inzicht in de prompts en monitor je promptdrift.


Na een productieve ochtend is het tijd voor de lunch. Dit is een ideaal moment voor een wandeling, een potje pingpong of om in onze Social Clubroom samen met collega’s te eten.

In de middag heb je een meeting met een klant uit de supply chain-sector. Samen met een Technology Specialist, een aantal Functioneel Consultants en een BI Consultant brainstorm je over manieren om backorders te optimaliseren met ATP in de keten. Met gerichte vragen ontdek je de kern van hun uitdagingen en vertaal je hun behoeften naar een passende AI-oplossing.


Om de dag af te ronden, bereid je je voor op de AI Development Day van morgen. Samen met Max (AI Business Line Expert) en Reuben & Lucas (AI Engineers) bedenk je welke nieuwe producten en diensten we kunnen ontwikkelen om onze klanten nog beter te bedienen.


Je kijkt er extra naar uit, want morgenmiddag ga je werken aan de doorontwikkeling van een bestaande AI-agent voor een klant in de manufacturing-sector. Daar zal je hun OpenAPI-specificatie exporteren en ombouwen in een MCP-server die de prestaties van de agent verbetert. Daarbij durf je nieuwe technologieën te verkennen en toe te passen.


Dit bieden wij jou

Onze mensen staan aan de basis van ons succes! Daarom doen we er alles aan om ervoor te zorgen dat iedereen het naar zijn zin heeft. Geen strakke hiërarchie, collega’s die elkaar graag helpen, ruimte en tijd voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling en kennisdeling: dat is bij Fellowmind vanzelfsprekend!


Natuurlijk bieden we jou ook een mooi pakket arbeidsvoorwaarden. Iedereen is anders en ook de wensen ten aanzien van arbeidsvoorwaarden lopen uiteen. Daarom is een deel van ons pakket flexibel en kun je bepaalde zaken inrichten naar je eigen wens.


Wat je in ieder geval kunt verwachten:

  • Een goed salaris, passend bij de functie die je gaat vervullen en de kennis en ervaring die je meeneemt.
  • Een aantrekkelijke bonusregeling gebaseerd op onze company performance.
  • 28 vakantiedagen op basis van een fulltime dienstverband. Je hebt de mogelijkheid om vakantiedagen bij te kopen of te verkopen van een apart flexbudget.
  • De mogelijkheid om 2x per jaar een lief & leed dag op te nemen waarin je de ruimte krijgt voor vrijwilligerswerk, mantelzorg of, bijvoorbeeld, een verhuizing.
  • Een gezonde balans tussen werken vanaf kantoor, op locatie bij onze klanten en thuis.
  • Bewegen is gezond! Maak een keuze uit onze aantrekkelijke sportieve regelingen zoals het leasen van een fiets of een bijdrage aan jouw sport abonnement.
  • Een inspirerende werkplek in Venlo, Deventer, Zwolle of Amersfoort waar je vkunt genieten van goede koffie en thee, fruit, soep en fris. Je bent van alle gemakken voorzien!
  • Reizen is een vast onderdeel van ons werk. Van huis naar klant, kantoor of naar een externe locatie. Voor iedere functie bieden we een passende regeling voor mobiliteit (lease of vergoeding).
  • We helpen je bij jouw unieke, persoonlijke ontwikkeling.
  • We geloven in focus en aandacht en daarom hebben we onze ‘focustijd’ ingericht. Tijdens de focustijd krijg je de ruimte om dingen te kunnen afronden, te werken aan jouw ontwikkeling of een kennissessie te bezoeken. Door slim te plannen zorgen we zo voor focus gedurende jouw werkdag.


Wie ben jij?

Voor het ontwikkelen van onze low- en pro-code AI-oplossingen gebruiken we uiteenlopende tools. Staar je echter niet blind op het lijstje hieronder: het is slechts een overzicht van kennis en ervaring waarmee je een vliegende start kunt maken:


  • Je hebt 3+ jaar ervaring als Data Scientist, Data Engineer, Machine Learning Engineer óf Software Engineer;
  • Je hebt ervaring met programmeren in C# en/of Python en gerelateerde (Gen.) AI libraries en frameworks (bv. Pyspark, Tensorflow/Keras, pytorch, SKlearn, Langchain, Autogen);
  • Je hebt affiniteit met het Microsoft platform (bv. Copilot Studio / Power Virtual Agents, Azure AI);
  • Je beheerst de Nederlandse- en Engelse taal in woord en geschrift;

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
You’ll join a multidisciplinary team of highly skilled developers, a Scrum Master, and a Product Owner. The team is technically strong and eager to understand and collaborate on the data science aspects of the work. Together, you’ll deliver solutions that make search smarter and integrate generative AI responsibly.

Our Area focuses on creating the best digital experience through a top-notch content platform, intuitive navigation, and powerful search. Your work will impact global websites, apps, and internal tools used by millions of customers and colleagues. As a Data Scientist, you’ll also play a key role in ensuring compliance through company's AI Way of Working, guiding the team through stage-gates and governance processes.










Responsibilities

· Develop and train machine learning models for semantic search and personalization.

· Fine-tune and evaluate embedding models and hybrid retrieval logic in Elasticsearch for search solutions.

· Research and implement LLM-based techniques for summarization, metadata generation and other use cases.

· Translate business questions into actionable data products and insights.

· Create clear dashboards and compelling storytelling to communicate insights and teach the team how to do this.

· Collaborate closely with developers, Scrum Master, and Product Owner to scale solutions effectively.

· Support compliance processes (Privacy Impact Assessments, model development plans) within the AI Way of Working.

· Monitor and improve model performance and explainability.




Qualifications

Must-Have Qualifications

· 3+ years of experience in machine learning and NLP.

· Expertise in information retrieval (semantic search, embeddings), and LLM integration.

· Familiarity with Elasticsearch and hybrid search architectures.

· Strong communication skills to explain complex concepts clearly.

· Ability to balance innovation with compliance and governance requirements.

· Knowledge of common evaluation methods and metrics (nDCG, MAP, MRR).

· Expertise in Python and working in (Databricks) notebooks.




Nice-to-Haves

· Experience with explainability and monitoring of ML models

· Knowledge of CI/CD and software development best practices

· Dashboarding and storytelling for insights

· Affinity with agile working and multidisciplinary collaboration

· Some familiarity with Java is appreciated




This is what you get




A 12-month assignment with the possibility of extension.
Working hours can be between 36 and 40 hours per week.
Hybrid working model: 1 to 2 days per week onsite, including 1 mandatory team day.
A maximum all-in annual salary of €90,000 based on a 40-hour work week (including 13th month and holiday allowance).
Opportunity to work within a large-scale, technology-driven enterprise environment.
The chance to shape and improve the quality strategy for mobile applications used by millions of customers.
A collaborative, agile, and innovation-focused work environment.
Inclusive and diverse workplace culture.
Competitive employment conditions through a detachment/contract employment structure (ZZP/freelance contractors are not eligible for this assignment).




For more information, please send an email to ritsa.paraskevopoulou@experis.nl or can contact her at +31638644015

Title: Data Scientist- Gen AI
Description:
ABOUT YOU

You are a Data Scientist with a passion for Generative AI and a strong engineering mindset. You enjoy turning business challenges into scalable AI solutions and thrive in collaborative environments where innovation, ownership, and continuous learning are encouraged. You are comfortable working across the full lifecycle of AI products, from experimentation and model development through to deployment and production support, while keeping business impact at the centre of your work.

WHAT ARE YOU GOING TO DO

* Design, build, test, deploy, and maintain scalable Generative AI solutions in production environments
* Work closely with business stakeholders to understand challenges and translate them into impactful AI-driven solutions
* Develop reusable GenAI components and contribute to the adoption of AI capabilities across multiple business domains
* Collaborate with engineers, product specialists, and fellow data scientists to deliver high-quality and responsible AI applications
* Contribute to MLOps, LLMOps, automation, monitoring, and continuous improvement of AI solutions

AN IDEAL PROFILE WOULD BE

* 5+ years of experience as a Data Scientist or in a similar role with strong Python development skills
* Experience with NLP, LLMs, Generative AI applications, and model development, deployment, and monitoring
* Strong engineering capabilities including Git, CI/CD, APIs, cloud platforms, containerisation, ETL processes, and database technologies
* Experience working with MLOps, LLMOps, DevOps, and cloud-based production environments such as Azure
* Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate across technical and non-technical stakeholders while coaching and sharing knowledge with others

Tick all the boxes? Then smash that button and let's have a chat.

ABOUT US:
Levy is an international IT staffing organization providing recruitment and project resourcing services to companies ranging from start-ups to well established global players across the UK, Holland, Germany, Belgium, and the USA. By partnering with our clients, we provide tailored interim and permanent IT staffing solutions to help them deliver their initiatives across applications and infrastructure, touching areas such as Digital, Data, Cloud, Cybersecurity and ERP.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist




We bouwen met Just Good People een nextgen data team met leuke mensen met sterke data, AI, human & biz skills. Geen PPT-consultancy, maar datakansen kraken en echt waarde realiseren bij toffe klanten. En veel opleiding.




Zie jij in bergen info – naast getallen – vooral kansen? Wil je die liever omzetten in actie dan in rapporten die in een la verdwijnen?




Wil je maximaal leren door in korte tijd bij meerdere bedrijven in de keuken te kijken? Terwijl wij je helpen ontwikkelen op het snijvlak van data, business en people?




Dan hebben we de uitdaging voor jou.




🟡 Just Good People

Bij JGP hebben we twee simpele regels: We nemen goede mensen aan. En we zorgen goed voor deze mensen. Dat betekent dat we samen optrekken om jou maximaal in je kracht te krijgen op een manier die bij jou past.







🌱 Unieke data filosofie

“As machines get better at being machines, humans have to get better at being more human.”

Deze nieuwe tijd vraagt om een nieuw soort data professional. Bij Just Good People Data helpen we bedrijven versnellen met top notch data professionals met goed ontwikkelde data, business én human skills. En trainen je naar nog hoger niveau. Met aandacht voor:




Volgende levels in Data | AI
“Waarde denken” voor klanten/ gebruikers
Stakeholder management
Communiceren & inspireren







⚡ Wat je gaat doen

Je werkt aan projecten bij mooie bedrijven zoals Bol.com, Defensie, Action, Rabo, NS, AMC, ASML, Essent.




Denk aan:




Maken van impact met data & AI
Stakeholders meenemen in de kracht van data – op een begrijpelijke manier
Verbinden van mensen, processen en tech
Ontwikkelen van voorspellende modellen, AI-oplossingen en slimme algoritmes
Experimenteren, leren en continu verbeteren op basis van data
Vertalen van complexe analyses naar heldere adviezen en concrete acties







👩 Wie je bent




Je bent technisch sterk maar ook nieuwsgierig naar de mens achter de vraag
Je hebt ervaring met machine learning, statistische modellering of AI-toepassingen
Je vindt het leuk om complexe vraagstukken simpel te maken
Je krijgt energie van verandering en nieuwe omgevingen
Je wilt blijven groeien in data, business én human skills
Je hebt 4+ jaar ervaring als Data Scientist
Je hebt een WO-opleiding, bijvoorbeeld in Data Science of Econometrie
Je bent ondernemend en denkt in zowel risico’s als kansen
Je schakelt gemakkelijk tussen strategie, business en data
Je durft vragen te stellen en bestaande werkwijzen uit te dagen
Je bent een echte teamspeler en communicatief sterk
Je spreekt en schrijft vloeiend Nederlands en Engels







🚀 Dit ga je krijgen

Vast dienstverband met goed salaris, bonusregeling en aantrekkelijke voorwaarden
Veel persoonlijke aandacht voor (waarde) groei, inzetbaarheid en “in je kracht komen“ als data professional. Met ook data-inhoudelijke mentoring.
Opleiding en kruisbestuiving met andere professionals in state of the art AI skills
De kans om in diverse keukens te kijken en veel te leren
Leaseauto of NS-Businesscard (ook privégebruik)
Gezellige borrels, kennissessies en team-events
Collega’s die naast slimme analyses ook goed zijn in slechte grappen




Wij zoeken goede data-mensen, die goede dingen doen. Van die types die je er gewoon heel graag bij wil hebben – net zo goed met mensen als met code!

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
As a Machine Learning Engineer, you will play a pivotal role in building systems that drive the training and deployment of large-scale ML models across our global operations. You'll collaborate with leading researchers, hardware experts, and software engineers to build robust solutions that maximize the potential of GPU acceleration, distributed computing, and the latest open-source tools. Your work will influence our trading strategies by accelerating experimentation cycles that foster continuous innovation and refinement.

This is a unique opportunity to solve problems at the intersection of advanced machine learning and trading, where your contributions will shape the future of IMC’s technology and trading capabilities.

Your Core Responsibilities


The opportunity to be in a brand new position in a growing team
Develop large-scale distributed training pipelines to manage datasets and complex models
Build and optimize low-latency inference pipelines, ensuring models deliver real-time predictions in production systems
Develop libraries to improve the performance of machine learning frameworks
Maximize performance in training and inference using GPU hardware and acceleration libraries
Design scalable model frameworks capable of handling high-volume trading data and delivering real-time, high-accuracy predictions
Collaborate with quantitative researchers to automate ML experiments, hyperparameter tuning, and model retraining
Partner with HPC specialists to optimize workflows, improve training speed, and reduce costs
Evaluate and roll out third-party tools to enhance model development, training, and inference capabilities
Dig into the internals of open-source ML tools to extend their capabilities and improve performance


Your Skills And Experience


5+ years of experience in machine learning with a focus on training or inference systems
Hands-on experience with real-time, low-latency ML pipelines in high-performance environments is a strong plus
Strong engineering skills, including Python, CUDA, or C++
Knowledge of machine learning frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, or JAX
Proficiency in GPU programming for training and inference acceleration (e.g., CuDNN, TensorRT)
Experience with distributed training for scaling ML workloads (e.g., Horovod, NCCL)
Exposure to cloud platforms and orchestration tools
A track record of contributing to open-source projects in machine learning, data science, or distributed systems is a plus


About Us

IMC is a global trading firm powered by a cutting-edge research environment and a world-class technology backbone. Since 1989, we’ve been a stabilizing force in financial markets, providing essential liquidity upon which market participants depend. Across our offices in the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, and India, our talented quant researchers, engineers, traders, and business operations professionals are united by our uniquely collaborative, high-performance culture, and our commitment to giving back. From entering dynamic new markets to embracing disruptive technologies, and from developing an innovative research environment to diversifying our trading strategies, we dare to continuously innovate and collaborate to succeed.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Overview

Company Description

VPR Energy BV is part of the Vitol Group, a global leader in the energy and commodities sector. With key operations in Houston, London, Singapore, Rotterdam, and 40 other offices worldwide, Vitol serves the world’s energy markets. Vitol invests globally in traditional and sustainable energy assets, including 18 million mm³ of storage, 850+ kbpd of refining capacity, and more than 7,000 service stations.

VPR Energy

Located in the Europoort-Rotterdam area, VPR Energy BV operates the most dynamic refinery owned by the world’s largest independent energy and commodities trading company. Our highly efficient and flexible 140 kbpd topping refinery processes gas condensate and crude oil into naphtha, jet fuel, gasoil, and residual fuel. These valuable products serve as feedstock in the petrochemical industry or as energy to heat homes and fuel marine vessels.

Every day, we leverage our expertise in refining crude and condensate blends, striving to be best in class in terms of energy reductions and sustainability targets. Our operations are managed from our office in Europoort Rotterdam by a small, highly engaged team with extensive knowledge and experience in processing, operations, and logistics. Our people build our reputation as a leading player in the energy markets through their creativity, commitment, professionalism, and entrepreneurial spirit.

As VPR Energy continues to grow steadily, we are seeking the next member to join our innovative team.

Job Description

Position Summary

The Data Scientist will act as an important support for the wider VPR performance team, using expertise to improve and enhance business activities. Core responsibilities include:


Writing and implementing algorithms to extract, structure, and analyze data
Applying existing software and tools, or developing new ones (including new data science methods and techniques) to transform unstructured, raw data into usable business information
Translating insights obtained from data into concrete recommendations for business operations in written reports, dashboards, or presentations
Managing and enhancing data streams and analyses
Automating tasks such as data collection


Qualifications

Qualifications and Experience


Minimum Bachelor’s degree in a STEM field (for example econometrics, engineering, or mathematics)
2 – 5 years of relevant working experience
Strong database programming skills (such as Python and SQL) and excellent knowledge of Excel
Experience with CI/CD processes (e.g., Git)
Ability to convey complex analysis in a concise and understandable manner
Enjoys working in a dynamic environment
A good command of the Dutch and English language

HTML:
Overview

Company Description

VPR Energy BV is part of the Vitol Group, a global leader in the energy and commodities sector. With key operations in Houston, London, Singapore, Rotterdam, and 40 other offices worldwide, Vitol serves the world’s energy markets. Vitol invests globally in traditional and sustainable energy assets, including 18 million mm³ of storage, 850+ kbpd of refining capacity, and more than 7,000 service stations.

VPR Energy

Located in the Europoort-Rotterdam area, VPR Energy BV operates the most dynamic refinery owned by the world’s largest independent energy and commodities trading company. Our highly efficient and flexible 140 kbpd topping refinery processes gas condensate and crude oil into naphtha, jet fuel, gasoil, and residual fuel. These valuable products serve as feedstock in the petrochemical industry or as energy to heat homes and fuel marine vessels.

Every day, we leverage our expertise in refining crude and condensate blends, striving to be best in class in terms of energy reductions and sustainability targets. Our operations are managed from our office in Europoort Rotterdam by a small, highly engaged team with extensive knowledge and experience in processing, operations, and logistics. Our people build our reputation as a leading player in the energy markets through their creativity, commitment, professionalism, and entrepreneurial spirit.

As VPR Energy continues to grow steadily, we are seeking the next member to join our innovative team.

Job Description

Position Summary

The Data Scientist will act as an important support for the wider VPR performance team, using expertise to improve and enhance business activities. Core responsibilities include:

  • Writing and implementing algorithms to extract, structure, and analyze data
  • Applying existing software and tools, or developing new ones (including new data science methods and techniques) to transform unstructured, raw data into usable business information
  • Translating insights obtained from data into concrete recommendations for business operations in written reports, dashboards, or presentations
  • Managing and enhancing data streams and analyses
  • Automating tasks such as data collection

Qualifications

Qualifications and Experience

  • Minimum Bachelor’s degree in a STEM field (for example econometrics, engineering, or mathematics)
  • 2 – 5 years of relevant working experience
  • Strong database programming skills (such as Python and SQL) and excellent knowledge of Excel
  • Experience with CI/CD processes (e.g., Git)
  • Ability to convey complex analysis in a concise and understandable manner
  • Enjoys working in a dynamic environment
  • A good command of the Dutch and English language
  • No 9-to-5 mentality

Additional Information

Additional Information

  • Contribute directly to a large variety of projects
  • Be part of a small and flat organization with lots of creative freedom
  • Free in-house lunch, freshly prepared and catered daily
  • Potential for travel to our Geneva and London office
  • Competitive compensation and benefits package

Title: Junior Data Scientist
Description:
Are you passionate about Artificial Intelligence and eager to turn emerging technologies into practical business solutions? Do you enjoy building innovative tools while also helping others understand and adopt new technologies? At Haskoning, we are looking for a Junior Data Scientist who combines strong analytical skills with a genuine interest in AI and Large Language Models (LLMs).

In this role, you will help accelerate AI adoption within our Supply Chain & Logistics advisory group, contribute to innovative client projects, and develop internal tools that improve the way we work. You will work at the intersection of data science, optimization, software development, and AI innovation.

Whether you're exploring the latest AI trends, developing prototypes, or supporting optimization projects for our clients, you will have the opportunity to make a tangible impact from day one.

What you will do as Junior Data Scientist

As a Junior Data Scientist, you support the team in identifying, developing, and implementing AI-driven solutions that create value both internally and for our clients.


Drive AI and LLM initiatives by keeping track of developments in AI tooling, platforms, and frameworks; monitoring internal AI solutions and identifying opportunities for reuse and improvement; exploring emerging AI technologies and translating trends into practical applications; developing prototypes and proof-of-concepts; and supporting the integration of AI and LLM capabilities into existing products and services
Develop tools and enable colleagues by building AI-powered tools that improve efficiency and productivity; contributing to solutions such as smart data ingestion platforms, AI-assisted code reviews, and intelligent assistants; helping define best practices for prompting, evaluation, governance, and AI use-case design; organizing workshops and knowledge-sharing sessions; and supporting colleagues in adopting AI technologies
Contribute to client projects within supply chain and logistics, including planning & optimization, cost-to-serve analyses, supply chain network design, and warehouse location and capacity studies
Perform data analyses and develop models in Python
Assist with validation, interpretation, communication of results, and explore opportunities to enhance existing solutions with AI and LLM capabilities


Where you will work

At Haskoning, you will join an independent, employee-owned international consultancy that combines engineering, design, and consultancy services with software and technology. Our mission, "Enhancing Society Together," drives us to create a positive impact on the world.

We offer purpose-driven and challenging work in diverse international projects, leveraging our 140+ years of expertise. With over 6,800 employees in more than 25 countries, we work together to turn challenges into opportunities and make a real impact on society. We collaborate with leading clients in markets such as infrastructure, climate resilience, renewable energy, decarbonisation of industry, and sustainable mobility.

As our new Junior Data Scientist, you will become part of Haskoning's Supply Chain & Logistics team, a multidisciplinary group of consultants, data scientists, and optimization specialists who help clients make smarter decisions through data and technology.

Our team works on complex challenges across industries, combining advanced analytics, operations research, software development, and domain expertise. At the same time, we are actively exploring how AI and LLM technologies can further enhance our services and transform the way we work.

You will collaborate closely with colleagues across advisory groups, AI initiatives, and digital teams, giving you exposure to a broad range of expertise and innovative projects.

We offer a collaborative and entrepreneurial environment where curiosity, continuous learning, and innovation are encouraged.

What you bring

You are an enthusiastic early-career professional who enjoys both building solutions and explaining them to others. You are curious about new technologies and motivated to translate innovative ideas into practical applications.


A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science & AI, Business Analytics, Operations Research, Computer Science, Econometrics, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field
Excellent communication skills in Dutch and English, with 0–4 years of relevant experience
Programming experience in Python and SQL, combined with strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Understanding of data pipelines and data modelling concepts, and knowledge of LLM concepts such as prompting, embeddings, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
A proactive attitude and eagerness to learn and inspire


It is a plus if you have experience with Azure cloud services; LLM frameworks such as LangChain, Semantic Kernel, or similar technologies; Git and software development best practices; supply chain and logistics analytics; mathematical optimization or operations research techniques; and facilitating workshops or delivering training sessions.

What you can expect from us

At Haskoning, we believe that the well-being of our employees is essential to our shared success. That’s why we offer a comprehensive benefits package designed to support both your professional development and personal well-being. 

What we offer: 


A solid foundation: A competitive base salary (€ 3.146 – € 4.493, based on experience and skills) with annual increases.
An attractive compensation package: Including a strong pension scheme with an 18.3% employer contribution, a 2.15% personal budget, and annual profit sharing. You’ll also have the opportunity to purchase company shares and share in our success. 
Work-life balance: 28 vacation days (based on full-time employment), with the option to purchase an additional 16.5 days per year and to exchange three public holidays for days that are personally meaningful to you. We work in a hybrid model with flexible hours, a home office allowance, and a fully equipped home workspace. 
Development opportunities: A wide range of training and development programs, including tailored learning paths, learning weeks, and various knowledge-sharing groups. 
Travel allowance: A conveniently located workplace at one of our offices, with travel expense reimbursement and an NS Business Card. If you walk or bike to work, you’ll receive a higher travel allowance—encouraging sustainable commuting. 
Informal and inclusive culture: A welcoming and open environment with voluntary social and sports activities, and employee networks such as Pride and Young Haskoning. 
Innovation exposure: The opportunity to contribute to forward-looking initiatives in AI and data-driven ways of working.


Read More About Our Employee Benefits Here. 

Are you our new colleague?

Then we would like to meet you! Click on the apply button and upload your resume and motivation letter. After receiving your application, we will contact you as soon as possible.

Do you have any questions about the role or the process? You can always contact Christian de Visser, Recruiter, at christian.de.visser@haskoning.com

At Haskoning, we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered. Our mission of "Enhancing Society Together" drives us to create an environment that embraces each individual’s unique background, identity, and perspective. Regardless of your experience, background, age, gender, or other personal characteristics—you are welcome here. We believe that diversity makes us stronger and that an inclusive culture gives everyone the opportunity to grow and contribute. Read more about our ED&I initiatives on our dedicated webpage.

Due to the privacy law, please submit your application via the apply button and not via email.

Acquisition for this vacancy by agencies is not appreciated and offered candidates will not be taken into consideration.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist

Location: Netherlands (Hybrid)

Salary: €90K - €120k + bonus

Contract: Fixed-Term

HL1106




Finatal are partnering with a leading international technology business to appoint a Senior Data Scientist. Sitting within a central data function, this role will focus on delivering end-to-end Data Science and AI initiatives across multiple business units and markets. You will take ownership of projects from opportunity identification and requirements gathering through to machine learning model development, AI and LLM implementation, production deployment and communicating business impact. Alongside the technical delivery, you will work closely with senior stakeholders to translate complex analysis into measurable commercial outcomes across growth, customer engagement, pricing and operational performance.




Role:

• Own the end-to-end delivery of Data Science and AI projects, from problem definition through to deployment and adoption.

• Build and implement machine learning models and analytical solutions that drive commercial outcomes across areas such as conversion, retention, pricing, and customer behaviour.

• Partner closely with Product, Growth, Commercial and Operational stakeholders to identify opportunities and prioritise initiatives.

• Translate complex technical findings into clear, actionable recommendations for business leaders and decision-makers.

• Design and implement AI, Machine Learning and LLM-driven solutions that improve decision-making and deliver measurable commercial outcomes.

• Contribute to the development of scalable Data Science and AI capabilities within a fast-paced, international environment.




Requirements:

• Strong hands-on Data Science experience with advanced Python and SQL skills, experience building and deploying Machine Learning models in production environments, and exposure to cloud platforms and large-scale data processing technologies such as PySpark.

• Experience working with AI, Generative AI and LLM-based applications, applying them to solve real business and operational challenges.

• Proven ability to own projects end-to-end, from problem definition and stakeholder engagement through to deployment and measurable business impact.

• Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with experience translating complex technical concepts into actionable recommendations.




If you are interested in learning more about this opportunity, please share your CV to Harriet at Harriet.lander@finatal.com.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Als Data Scientist bij i4talent werk je aan opdrachten bij organisaties in de Randstad. Je verdient tussen de €3.500 en €5.720 bruto per maand en komt in dienst bij i4talent, terwijl je als consultant werkt aan voorspellende modellen, machine learning, Python en cloudomgevingen zoals Azure of AWS.




Je werkt op projecten waar data echt verschil kan maken. Denk aan forecasting, klantgedrag, risicoanalyse, optimalisatie of automatisering. Wij kijken samen welke opdracht past bij jouw ervaring, interesses en gewenste ontwikkelrichting.




Jij als gangmaker

Voor jou is een model geen einddoel, maar een middel om betere beslissingen te nemen. Je bent nieuwsgierig naar patronen in data, maar ook kritisch op de toepasbaarheid van modellen in de praktijk. Je vertaalt complexe analyses naar begrijpelijke inzichten en weet stakeholders mee te nemen in wat een model doet, waarom het werkt en wat de impact is op hun business.




Wat ga je doen?

Als Data Scientist werk je aan uiteenlopende data science vraagstukken binnen cloud-gedreven omgevingen.

Je houdt je onder andere bezig met:

Ontwikkelen van voorspellende modellen en machine learning-oplossingen met Python.
Toepassen van algoritmes voor forecasting, classificatie, clustering en optimalisatie.
Analyseren van complexe datasets en vertalen naar bruikbare inzichten.
Operationaliseren van modellen in cloudomgevingen zoals Azure, AWS of GCP
Samenwerken met Data Engineers om data pipelines en feature sets beschikbaar te maken.
Sparren met stakeholders over businessvraagstukken en data science toepassingen.
Verbeteren en monitoren van model performance in productie-omgevingen (MLOps waar van toepassing).
Afhankelijk van de opdracht werk je met technologieën zoals: Python (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn), TensorFlow of PyTorch (afhankelijk van opdracht), SQL voor data-extractie en
analyse, Azure Machine Learning / AWS SageMaker, Databricks voor data science en modelontwikkeling, Cloud data platformen zoals Azure , AWS of GCP) en Git en CI/CD pipelines (waar van toepassing)




Wat krijg je van i4talent?

Salaris tussen €3.500 en €5.720 bruto per maand.
Contract voor onbepaalde tijd.
Leaseauto met tankpas, ook privé te gebruiken.
25 vakantiedagen en 8% vakantiegeld.
Goede pensioenregeling, waarbij i4talent 4/5 van de premie betaalt.
Toegang tot de i4talent Academy met online en klassikale trainingen (inhoud en soft skills) via onder andere NIVE en Coursera, inclusief erkende certificaten.
Persoonlijke begeleiding van een Fieldmanager en Happy professional manager.
Afwisselende opdrachten bij fintechs, logistieke bedrijven, corporates en scale-ups.
Events: van inhoudelijke sessies tot trips, bootdagen en borrels.




Wat breng jij mee?

Je hebt een sterke analytische basis en weet hoe je data science praktisch toepast binnen organisaties.

Daarnaast breng je mee:

Een hbo- of wo-diploma in een kwantitatieve richting (bijv. Data Science, AI, Wiskunde, Econometrie).
Minimaal 2-3 jaar ervaring als Data Scientist of in een vergelijkbare analytische/ ML rol.
Sterke ervaring met Python en data science libraries.
Ervaring met supervised en unsupervised machine learning technieken.
Ervaring met cloudplatformen zoals Azure of AWS.
Begrip van modelvalidatie, performance metrics en datavoorbereiding.
Goede communicatieve vaardigheden en vermogen om complexe materie uit te leggen.
Vloeiende beheersing van de Nederlandse taal.
Rijbewijs en bereidheid om te reizen binnen de Randstad.
