Title: Trading Analyst
Description:
Join us, be part of more.

We’re so much more than an energy company. We’re a family of brands revolutionising how we power the planet. We're energisers. One team of 21,000 colleagues that’s energising a greener, fairer future by creating an energy system that doesn’t rely on fossil fuels, whilst living our powerful commitment to igniting positive change in our communities. Here, you can find more purpose, more passion and more potential. That’s why working here is #MoreThanACareer. We do energy differently – we do it all. We make it, store it, move it, sell it and mend it.

About your team:

At Centrica Energy, our mission is to move it.

We’re energy movers by nature. We’re a global renewable energy trading company which helps move energy from source to use – powering businesses, homes, and societies as they transition to a new sustainable energy future. If the idea of working to create a sustainable energy future also moves you, we may very well be the right place for you.

A rare opportunity to shape trading decisions in a business operating at the intersection of analytics, optimisation and energy markets. This role brings together quantitative thinking, model development and close collaboration with traders and portfolio managers to improve performance, uncover market opportunities and support the optimisation of renewables, batteries and thermal assets across Europe, the UK and Ireland.

The team 

Trading Analytics is part of the Short-Term Asset Trading team, responsible for trading our renewables, battery portfolio and thermal plants across continental Europe and UK & Ireland. 

We maximise the value of Centrica Energy’s asset portfolio by: 


Delivering market, portfolio and performance insights. 
Improving trading strategies. 
Increasing automation. 
Driving the integration of algorithmic trading. 


The team plays a central role in the organisation and works closely with the trading team, algo development teams, portfolio management and commercial teams. This role is particularly well suited for people with a broad interest in our activities and commercial mindset.

About The Role

This role sits close to the commercial heartbeat of the business, providing analysis that helps translate complex market and operational dynamics into clear decision support. The Trading Analyst will work across data, modelling and trading activity to strengthen performance understanding and support better optimisation outcomes. It is a role for someone who enjoys combining quantitative rigour with commercial relevance in a fast-moving energy environment.


Support the optimisation and trading of a portfolio spanning renewable, battery and thermal assets across continental Europe and the UK & Ireland.
Deliver analysis and decision support to traders and portfolio managers, helping explain performance, identify market opportunities and assess risk.
Develop analytical models and tools that improve trading strategies, asset optimisation and forecasting capabilities.
Explore market and operational data to uncover value drivers, explain outcomes and identify new opportunities for value creation.
Contribute to the continued expansion of automation and algorithmic trading within the Short-Term Asset Trading team.


About You

The ideal candidate combines a structured, analytical mindset with the ability to work hands-on with data and quantitative models. A strong foundation in statistics, optimisation and programming is complemented by a curiosity for understanding energy markets and trading dynamics. Success in the role depends on the ability to translate complex problems into actionable insights while collaborating effectively across teams.

We are looking for someone who has:


A few years of relevant experience from a similar role.
Strong analytical capabilities, with a proven ability to explore data in depth and explain underlying drivers of performance.
Experience translating real-world challenges into mathematical optimisation problems and evaluating solution quality.
Solid understanding of probability, statistical distributions, and experience with back-testing and performance validation of algorithms.
Proficiency in Python development, including practical experience leveraging modern AI-assisted coding tools.
Experience in, or a clear interest in, power markets, renewables or energy systems, combined with strong communication skills and a collaborative mindset.


About Centrica Energy

Centrica Energy is a global energy trading and optimisation business that helps move energy from source to use, connecting producers, suppliers and offtakers across wholesale energy markets. The business trades power, gas, liquefied natural gas and green certificates, and operates from eight offices across three continents with the purpose of energising a greener, fairer future.

The business is one of the largest renewable asset managers in Europe, with more than 18.8 GW of capacity under contract, of which more than 85% is renewable assets. Alongside trading activity, Centrica Energy also provides optimisation and flexibility services across batteries, renewable flexibility, combined heat and power plants and other flexible assets, using advanced algorithms, market expertise and 24/7 operations to maximise value across major power markets.

Power trading activity spans major exchanges and OTC markets across Europe and the United States, covering intraday, day-ahead and longer-dated products, supported by advanced algorithmic trading capabilities. As the trading and optimisation arm of Centrica plc, Centrica Energy plays an important role in managing commodity risk, supporting the green transition and helping create more resilient and efficient energy systems.

Why should you apply?

We’re not a perfect place – but we’re a people place. Our priority is supporting all of the different realities our people face. Life is about so much more than work. We get it. That’s why we’ve designed our total rewards to give you the flexibility to choose what you need, when you need it, making sure that you and your family are supported not only financially, but physically and emotionally too. Visit the link below to discover why we’re a great place to work and what being part of more means for you.

https://www.morethanacareer.energy/centricaenergy

If you're full of energy, fired up about sustainability, and ready to craft not only a better tomorrow, but a better you, then come and find your purpose in a team where your voice matters, your growth is non-negotiable, and your ambitions are our priority.

Help us, help you. We would love for you to share any information about yourself throughout our recruitment process so that we can better understand you and help shape your journey.

Title: Data Science & BI Developer - 12 month maternity cover
Place: Hørsholm, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Join our Golf Insights team and work hands-on with one of the world’s richest data ecosystems in golf. You’ll be appointed to specific product segments and deliver insights that foster data-driven decisions internally and external customers.

Our team is responsible for the transformational data modelling utilized in different consumption layers, driving meaningful accessible insights. We are on a continuous journey to strengthen our foundation of augmented analytics, which you will be expected to contribute to.

We collaborate closely with the product teams as well as various R&D functions, to deliver insights that drive product development and customer business success.

Your Profile

We Are Looking For a Data Science & BI Developer To Join Our Team On a 12-month Maternity Cover Contract. We Expect You To Have 3+ Years Of Experience In BI And Analytics Or Related Fields. Golf Knowledge And Passion For The Sport Is a Plus, While We Also Expect That You Are


Motivated, proactive, and a fast learner 
Curious about working with complex data 
Enthusiastic about using AI tools to work smarter and build better
Excited by the idea of building high-quality BI tools that have a real-world impact 
Eager to grow in an international, collaborative, and sports-driven environment 


You have some hands-on experience with SQL and dashboards (Power BI or similar), and ideally some exposure to Microsoft Fabric. You’re comfortable working with data from initial transformations to final reports, and you're energized by the opportunity to bring AI into how insights are created and consumed. 

Responsibilities


Build and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports 
Leverage AI development tools to accelerate BI deliverables and improve output quality
Explore and implement augmented analytics capabilities to enhance how end users interact with data
Develop and optimize data models and schemas 
Collaborate with data engineers to acquire and prepare data 
Work with product owners and internal stakeholders to define BI needs 


Job Requirements


A master's degree in Business Intelligence, Computer Science, Data Analytics or a similar data-related field
Experience writing SQL and working with relational databases 
Proficiency with Power BI or other dashboarding tools, while broader experience in Microsoft Fabric is a plus
Experience with dbt
A proactive, independent approach – you enjoy owning your tasks and learning as you go 
Fluent in English and comfortable working in a cross-cultural team 


The Office

We are located at DTU Science Park in Hørsholm. We take great pride in the state-of-art building we have designed! The office features bright meeting rooms, coffee areas to catch up with colleagues, a large testing arena with simulators available for employees and a fitness center, everything surrounded by a beautiful forest and respecting the environment. We made sure to utilize the newest technologies to be energy efficient and be mindful of ventilation and sound propagation. The cherry on top is our canteen: with an indoor and outdoor area to enjoy the fresh food prepared by our own kitchen staff.

Join the home of a powerful sports brand and a one-of-a-kind technology

Our proprietary technology is based on expert knowledge about radar, computer vision, data, and software engineering. Our solutions are developed by specialists who endlessly explore and challenge new technical boundaries. Trackman’s blend of cutting-edge technology, sports, and continued growth make our company an outstanding place to work.

Our work culture is entrepreneurial, ambitious, and rewarding as you get to collaborate with inspiring colleagues and interact with the leaders of golf, baseball, and football. Based on a unique brand reputation, we are proud to work in close partnership with the top athletes, organizations, and teams that rely on our technology to stay ahead of the race.

We are a Danish company founded, owned, and managed since 2003 by three entrepreneurs with a strong passion for sports. Today, we have a global footprint with our headquarters located in Denmark (Hørsholm) and offices in New York, Phoenix, Seoul and Tokyo. We employ over 1000+ people worldwide, half of which work in Denmark, where most hardware and software products are developed, tested, and manufactured.

Where innovation happens

At Trackman, we know that great people make great products. We believe that teams thrive, and innovation sparks in an atmosphere where people are encouraged to think for themselves, where everybody’s voices are heard, and where the best ideas prevail in the pursuit of exceptional solutions. This mix makes Trackman an inspiring company – for our customers and colleagues.

Share our Passion!

Applications are continuously assessed, so please send your application as soon as possible. For additional information about the position, please contact hiring manager; Mikkel Hansen at mha@trackman.com .

Title: Quantitative Analyst
Description:
Do you want to gain a deep understanding of the power market and contribute directly to trading decisions? Do you thrive in a high‑paced, data‑driven environment where your analytical skills shape tomorrow’s strategies? If you’re ready for a role with real responsibility and impact, join our team as a Quantitative Analyst and help drive disciplined, high‑quality trading outcomes.




We are seeking a highly motivated individual with strong quantitative skills to join our team as a Quantitative Analyst. In this role, you will work closely with the team, applying data‑driven insights, modelling, and risk thinking to take ownership of disciplined trading decisions. Your ability to transform analysis and code into actionable insights will directly contribute to improving the quality and profitability of our trading decisions.




In this role, you will gain deep insights into our trading strategies and tools that provide a significant competitive advantage. You can expect a steep learning curve and growth opportunities in an environment where flat hierarchy and team spirit drive our success. You will experience a high degree of autonomy and see your own ideas come to life and see your ideas quickly evolve into tangible solutions that make a meaningful impact on our trading operations.




Key Responsibilities

Market Analysis: Conduct in-depth, data‑driven analysis of power markets including supply and demand fundamentals, regulatory developments, weather‑driven patterns, and geopolitical impacts to uncover actionable trading opportunities and support strategic decision‑making.
Risk Management: Implement robust risk management practices by monitoring position limits, conducting stress testing, and assessing exposure to volatile market conditions to ensure controlled and compliant trading activities.
Tool Development: Develop, maintain, and optimize a range of analytical, real‑time monitoring, and reporting tools that enhance trading decision‑making and operational efficiency. Build automated dashboards and data pipelines to produce accurate, timely reports on trading performance, risk metrics, and key market trends.
Model Development: Continuously improve trading models through back testing and market‑driven data analysis to strengthen and support profitable trading decisions.
Collaboration: Work closely with the team to enhance trading strategies and technical setup, ensuring clear and consistent communication across all team members.
Trading Application: Apply insights from analysis, modelling and risk assessment to confidently support and execute well‑founded trading decisions that meet all required risk, compliance, and performance standards.




Qualifications and Requirements

As a candidate, we expect you:

Hold a bachelor’s or master’s degree in mathematics‑economics, economics, statistics, engineering, or another quantitative field.
Have strong quantitative and analytical skills to interpret complex data and translate it into well‑founded trading insights.
Are proficient in Python and comfortable with coding. Experience with other code languages is considered an advantage.
Have a strong interest in financial and energy markets and a desire to deepen your understanding of power‑market dynamics.
Demonstrate excellent problem‑solving skills, high attention to detail, and the ability to adapt quickly to changing market conditions.
Communicate clearly and effectively, presenting technical and analytical concepts in a way that is easy for both technical and non‑technical team members to understand.




What We Offer

As an employer, we offer:

A full-time position that gives you the opportunity to apply your acquired skills.
Support in your role from experienced professionals.
Access to unique technologies and resources.
An opportunity to work at the forefront of power trading, contributing to groundbreaking strategies.
Opportunities for career growth and advancement.
Competitive compensation package.
A modern office in the heart of Aarhus.
Professional and committed colleagues with high ambitions.
Inspiring, collaborative, and innovative work environment.
A place where your contributions are recognized and have a meaningful impact on the company's success.




A Profitable Growth Company with a Clear Vision

ENZEE is an ambitious growth company specialized in energy trading across the European continent. We envision a sustainable world and therefore, every day at ENZEE is a day with purpose. We belief that well-functioning and efficient energy markets are a necessity towards a net zero future which is exactly where we contribute with our expertise.




The ENZEE team consists of highly qualified, experienced, and knowledgeable industry professionals with a wide range of expertise in the energy markets. As a member of our team, you can expect a steep learning curve, great career growth opportunities, and the possibility of making a significant contribution.




Jobstart & Type

As soon as possible. We will be screening candidates on an ongoing basis
Full-time position
Location: Aarhus




ENZEE is dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace and therefore we encourage all candidates to apply, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, sexual orientation, or disabilities.




For further inquiries, please contact Akeel Elhakim: +45 53 85 80 37

Title: Data Engineer - Onsite Copenhagen
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Data Engineer

AI is changing how software gets built.

The data underneath it matters more than ever.

At Heyra, we build AI systems, workflows and data platforms that are meant to live in production from day one. We work closely with companies that are moving quickly and need infrastructure that can keep up with the pace of change.

We’re looking for a Data Engineer who enjoys building things properly. Someone who can move through complex systems, shape clean architecture and create the foundations modern AI workflows depend on.

You’ll work closely with engineers, AI specialists, strategists and designers in a company where ideas move quickly and the distance from thinking to building is intentionally small.

What You’ll Work On


Building modern data platforms and pipelines
Creating semantic foundations for AI systems
Developing AI-powered workflows and agentic systems
Helping clients move AI into real operational environments
Working closely with both technical teams and decision makers
Shaping how modern AI infrastructure is built in practice


You probably have experience with


dbt, SnowFlake, Databricks, Fabric, BigQuery or similar ecosystems
SQL, Python and APIs
Data modelling and warehousing
GenAI, ML or MLOps workflows
Complex systems and problem solving


Curiosity, taste and builder mentality matter just as much to us as specific tools or years of experience.

Why Heyra

At Heyra, we believe consulting needs to be rewritten. We're building a consultancy designed for the agentic era. We design, build, and operate AI products that change how enterprises work - from problem to prototype to production.

We care deeply about craft, speed and building things that work in the real world.

Heyra brings together people from engineering, strategy, product, startup and creative environments. We keep teams small, collaboration close and momentum high.

You’ll Join a Team Where


people care deeply about what they build
good ideas move quickly
new technology gets explored early
and where there is room to shape both the company and the way we work


We’re around 45 people today after merging with Pyne, and we’re only just getting started.

If this sounds interesting, send us a short note about yourself together with your CV. We care much more about curiosity, energy and the way you think than polished applications.

Title: AI Engineer
Description:
Location: Copenhagen

Reports to: Director of AI




The Opportunity

Dawn Health is a leading Digital Health product company that empowers Life Science companies to create elegantly designed digital solutions quickly and in compliance with the highest quality and reliability standards. We are looking for a AI/ML Engineer to join our team in Copenhagen.




You'll join our core platform team shipping features into live regulated products, working shoulder-to-shoulder with engineers, clinicians, product managers, and designers. If you want your work to reach real patients - this is the role.




Role Overview and Responsibilities

Design and ship AI-powered features - RAG pipelines, conversational interfaces, AI-assisted data interpretation.
Own end-to-end AI pipelines: prompt engineering, orchestration, output evaluation, and production monitoring.
Build and maintain data pipelines for structured and unstructured data; define data requirements as part of AI feature design.
Design evaluation frameworks and run automated benchmarks using standard metrics
Build AI systems with clear architecture - clean input/output contracts, REST APIs
Contribute to AI verification & validation (V&V) documentation and compliance activities within our ISO 13485 / SaMD compatible environment.
Identify and communicate AI/ML-related product and compliance risks; provide SME input to support decision-making.




Your Mindset

You are driven by purpose and genuinely motivated to improve patients' lives through technology.
You are AI-native - you actively experiment with new models, frameworks, and tooling to work faster and build better.
You explain technical complexity clearly to non-technical peers and write documentation others actually want to read.
You are a collaborative team player who shares knowledge freely and helps others grow.




What Success Looks Like (6-12 Months)

You have independently owned and shipped a RAG pipeline or LLM-powered feature that is actively used across one or more Dawn Health products.
Your observability and benchmarking setup gives the team real visibility into model performance in production, and you are driving iterative improvements based on the data.
Leveraging established Dawn Health templates and AI-augmented workflows to improve efficiency and impact.
Clients and cross-functional teams view you as a trusted, proactive partner.




About You

3-5 years of ML/AI engineering experience, with at least 1-2 years working with AI/ML systems in production.
Strong Python; hands-on with LLM APIs, prompt engineering, and RAG patterns. Familiarity with LangChain/LangGraph, LlamaIndex, or equivalent.
MLOps fluency: CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions / Azure Pipeline) and observability tools (e.g. LangFuse / LangSmith).
Solid ML fundamentals: supervised/unsupervised/RL, model evaluation, interpretability, and bias mitigation.
Familiar with regulated environments: EU AI Act, ISO 27001, OWASP GenAI Top 10, and pharma-specific processes.
Relevant Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, ML, or equivalent practical experience.
Bonus: fine-tuning open-source LLMs, digital biomarkers / PROs, AI agent frameworks, or medical device context.




What We Offer

A portfolio of meaningful products that impact patient lives at scale.
Autonomy, trust, and support to do the best work of your career.
An open, collaborative environment with talented peers across engineering, design, clinical, and regulatory.
Career growth in one of the leading digital health scale-ups, alongside world-class pharma partners.
An array of benefits within the areas of culture, professional development, and a competitive health package.
A social culture with running, book, winter bathing, knitting, and crossfit clubs to join
Aaaand, probably the best lunch in Copenhagen, and the office view to go with it ☕




About Dawn Health

Dawn Health is a digital health company specializing in developing Software as Medical Device (SaMD) and other regulated healthcare solutions. We deliver both strategic consulting and development of innovative digital therapies targeting patients on a global scale.

Working at Dawn Health is more than just a job. We are not just looking for new employees. We are looking for deeply passionate souls, gifted minds, innovators, team players, entrepreneurs and people who believe they can change the world.




We love to build things, change the status quo, and drive a new digital health agenda that will impact 1 million patients by 2030. Our passion not only includes the urge to make a difference and Code to Save Lives - we're also very focused on our people. You’ll find warm, helpful, and friendly colleagues who care about each other and the mission we’re on.

Title: DATA SCIENCE CONSULTANT COPENHAGEN
Description:
We are currently recruiting for our Summer 2026 intake, with an expected start date in September 2026.




Management Solutions is an international consulting firm whose core mission is to deliver business, risk, financial, organisational and process-related advisory services, targeting both functional aspects and the implementation of related technologies. We currently have a multidisciplinary team (functional, mathematical, technical and systems integration) of 4,000 professionals.

We operate through 52 offices (24 in Europe, 23 in the Americas, 3 in Asia, 1 in Africa and 1 in Oceania) from where we regularly serve clients that operate in more than 50 countries across five major geographical areas (Europe, Americas, Asia, Africa and Oceania).




Role:




Statistical data processing (Data Mining)
Artificial Intelligence (AI), including generative AI
Predictive modeling using Machine Learning and Data Science techniques
Time-series modeling (ARIMA and panel data models) and simulation models (Monte Carlo)
Development, validation and audit of financial, risk, marketing and business-oriented models
R&D projects




Requirements:




Recent graduates or final year students.
Should desirably have knowledge of Artificial Intelligence modeling techniques (logit, GLM, time series, random forests, clustering, XGBoost), statistical programming languages (SAS, R, Python, Matlab), big data tools and platforms (Hadoop, Hive, etc.) and cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP).
Solid academic record.
Strong computer skills.
Postgraduate studies and/or specialized courses are an asset, especially in Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Quantitative Finance or similar.
Knowledge of other languages is desirable.
Get-up-and-go attitude, maturity, responsibility and strong work ethic.
Strong ability to learn quickly.
Able to integrate easily into multidisciplinary teams.




What we offer:




We offer you the possibility to join a firm that provides all you need to develop your talent to the fullest:




Working in the highest-profile consulting projects in the industry,
for the largest companies, leaders of their respective markets,
alongside top industry management as they face challenges at the national and global level,
as part of an extraordinary team of professionals whose values and corporate culture are a benchmark for the industry




Training:

Ongoing training plan, with approximately 10% of business turnover spent on training.
Specialist knowledge courses, external expert courses, professional skills courses and language courses.
Last year our staff as a whole received over 400,000 hours of training spanning more than 160 courses.




Career plan:

Clearly defined career plan.
Internal promotion based solely on merit.
Partnership-based management model offers all professionals the opportunity to become part of the Firm’s group of partners




Complementary experiences:




University: we maintain a close relationship with the world’s most prestigious universities.
Social Action: we organize more than 30 community support activities.
Sports Club: internal and external tournaments.




How to apply:




If you are interested in our offer, please complete our application form online at https://www.managementsolutions.com/en/professional-careers-send-us-your-cv

  • Should desirably have knowledge of Artificial Intelligence modeling techniques (logit, GLM, time series, random forests, clustering, XGBoost), statistical programming languages (SAS, R, Python, Matlab), big data tools and platforms (Hadoop, Hive, etc.) and cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP).
  • Solid academic record.
  • Strong computer skills.
  • Postgraduate studies and/or specialized courses are an asset, especially in Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Quantitative Finance or similar.
  • Knowledge of other languages is desirable.
  • Get-up-and-go attitude, maturity, responsibility and strong work ethic.
  • Strong ability to learn quickly.
  • Able to integrate easily into multidisciplinary teams.


What we offer:


We offer you the possibility to join a firm that provides all you need to develop your talent to the fullest:


  • Working in the highest-profile consulting projects in the industry,
  • for the largest companies, leaders of their respective markets,
  • alongside top industry management as they face challenges at the national and global level,
  • as part of an extraordinary team of professionals whose values and corporate culture are a benchmark for the industry


Training:

  • Ongoing training plan, with approximately 10% of business turnover spent on training.
  • Specialist knowledge courses, external expert courses, professional skills courses and language courses.
  • Last year our staff as a whole received over 400,000 hours of training spanning more than 160 courses.


Career plan:

  • Clearly defined career plan.
  • Internal promotion based solely on merit.
  • Partnership-based management model offers all professionals the opportunity to become part of the Firm’s group of partners


Complementary experiences:


  • University: we maintain a close relationship with the world’s most prestigious universities.
  • Social Action: we organize more than 30 community support activities.
  • Sports Club: internal and external tournaments.


How to apply:


If you are interested in our offer, please complete our application form online at https://www.managementsolutions.com/en/professional-careers-send-us-your-cv

Title: Senior Applied Scientist - Computer Vision for Quantum
Place: Kongens Lyngby, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Overview

Join Microsoft Quantum to help build the foundation for scalable quantum computing by transforming experimental data into actionable engineering insight. You will work at the intersection of physics, software engineering, and cloud-scale data science, collaborating with scientists and engineers to accelerate quantum machine development. Your work will directly impact how we understand, evaluate, and improve quantum machines all the way up to utility scale.

At Microsoft Quantum, we aim to empower science and scientists to solve the world’s biggest problems by realizing advanced computing platforms at the intersection of high-performance computing, artificial intelligence, and quantum information technology. Microsoft Quantum will change the world of computing and help solve some of humankind’s currently unsolvable problems. For more information about our team, visit https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/quantum.

We are seeking a Senior Applied Scientist, to design, build, and scale analytical pipelines and algorithms that translate complex experimental measurements into decision-ready insights. You will work hands-on with imaging and characterization data, developing robust, production-grade analysis workflows that enable engineering teams to evaluate devices, improve yield, and iterate quickly. This opportunity will allow you to deepen your expertise at the intersection of software engineering and experimental science, develop scalable data and machine learning systems, and directly influence next-generation computing technologies.

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.

Responsibilities


Develop and maintain scalable analysis pipelines and agentic workflows for experimental microscopy imaging and characterization data, enabling consistent and reliable interpretation of the data.
Design and implement algorithms for image analysis, registration, feature extraction, and quantitative metrics, ensuring robustness, correctness, and reproducibility in production environments.
Build and operationalize machine learning and AI models for feature extraction, classification, regression, or anomaly detection, ensuring interpretability, validation, and long-term reliability.
Partner with physicists, hardware engineers, materials scientists, and data engineering teams to translate experimental data into actionable insights that improve product quality and yield.
Design and maintain clear, interpretable data models and datasets, including handling of missing, invalid, and unprocessed data states to ensure reproducibility.
Own the quality, reliability, and documentation of analytical workflows, including debugging, testing, performance tuning, and continuous improvement of production systems.
Embody our culture and values.  


Qualifications

Required Qualifications


Master’s or Doctorate degree in Computer Science, Physics, Electrical Engineering, Applied Mathematics, Statistics, Data Science, or a related field OR equivalent experience
Programming experience in Python, including scientific computing and development of production-quality code (testing, packaging, version control, and code review).
Experience with image processing, computer vision, or imaging-based analysis workflows.
Demonstrated expertise in statistical analysis and uncertainty estimation in measurement or experimental systems.


Additional Or Preferred Qualifications


Experience developing, training, validating, and deploying machine learning models for experimental or production data.
Experience building and maintaining data pipelines in modern distributed or analytics environments (e.g., Spark, Databricks, Delta Lake, or equivalent).
Familiarity with nanofabrication processes and associated metrology techniques.
Experience working in shared, long-lived analytical or production codebases.
Experience collaborating with experimental, hardware, or scientific teams and reasoning about physical system constraints.


Other Requirements 


Ability to meet Microsoft, customer and/or government security screening requirements are required for this role. These requirements include, but are not limited to the following specialized security screenings:   
Microsoft Cloud Background Check: This position will be required to pass the Microsoft Cloud Background Check upon hire/transfer and every two years thereafter. 
Citizenship & Citizenship Verification: This role will require access to information that is controlled for export under export control regulations, potentially under the U.S. International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) or Export Administration Regulations (EAR), the EU Dual Use Regulation, and/or other export control regulations. As a condition of employment, the successful candidate will be required to provide either proof of their country of citizenship or proof of their U.S. permanent residency or other protected status (e.g., under 8 U.S.C.
1324b(a)(3)) for assessment of eligibility to access the export-controlled information. To meet this legal requirement, and as a condition of employment, the successful candidate’s citizenship will be verified with a valid passport. Lawful permanent residents, refugees, and asylees may verify status using other documents, where applicable.  
Ability to leverage AI tools to drive innovation and efficiency (e.g., performance modeling and analysis, research gathering, day to day task automation).
Ability to work in an “AI-first” environment using modern AI tools to accelerate discovery through hardware development

Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. Title: Junior Quantitative Analyst
Description:
About Odigo Energy

Odigo Energy is a fast-growing energy trading company headquartered in Sydhavnen, Aarhus C. Our team of traders, analysts, and software developers is building a state-of-the-art trading infrastructure to drive innovation in the energy markets. With 13 full-time employees, we operate in a highly collaborative environment where quantitative research, technology, and trading come together to create measurable impact.

We are now looking for an entry-level / junior quantitative analyst who has recently graduated or is about to complete their studies this summer and wants to begin their career in algorithmic energy trading.




Role Overview

We are seeking a curious and driven graduate who is excited to apply mathematics, statistics, and programming skills to real-world financial markets. You will join a team that values initiative, independent thinking, and technical rigor. We provide deep domain knowledge in energy markets. You bring strong quantitative foundations and a willingness to learn fast.

This is a full-time position designed for someone at the beginning of their career who wants steep learning, real responsibility, and direct exposure to live trading environments.




Responsibilities

Develop and improve algorithmic trading strategies and forecasting models using real-time and historical data
Contribute to the design and expansion of internal trading and forecasting frameworks in Python
Analyze large datasets to identify market inefficiencies and trading opportunities
Collaborate closely with traders and developers to translate research into production systems
Continuously monitor and evaluate model performance in live markets




Qualifications

Recently completed or about to complete a degree in Mathematics, Physics, Economics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related quantitative field
Strong programming skills in Python. Experience with version control and structured software development practices is highly valued
Solid foundation in statistics, probability theory, and numerical methods
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities
Ability to work independently while contributing effectively to a small, high-performance team
Interest in financial markets and algorithmic trading




What We Offer

A full-time entry-level role in a fast-growing energy trading company
Direct exposure to live trading strategies and decision-making
Significant responsibility from day one
Rapid professional development in quantitative trading and energy markets
A collaborative environment where technical excellence and initiative are rewarded
The opportunity to grow with the company as it scales




If you are motivated to start your career in quantitative trading and want to work in a highly technical, impact-driven environment, we would love to hear from you.

Title: Quantitative Analyst in Credit Risk Model Validation
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Credit Risk Model Validation (CRMV) is hiring for a Risk Methodology & Model Validation specialist to support the validation of credit risk models across Danske Bank.

CRMV is responsible for independent validation of a broad range of credit risk models, including probability of default (PD), loss given default (LGD), credit conversion factor (CF), IFRS 9 and credit decision models. The team operates within Danske Bank's broader model risk management (MRM) framework and works closely with model developers, model owners and other stakeholders across the organisation.

We are looking for colleague who can combine quantitative judgement, structured problem-solving and clear communication with a practical interest in improving how validation work is performed. Depending on the role, this may include direct validation execution, contribution to technical tooling and automation, or leadership in shaping more standardised validation approaches.

This position could be based in Vilnius, Lithuania or Copenhagen, Denmark.

You will:


Execute and lead validations with increasing capability to manage your own work, perform structured quantitative analysis and translate findings into clear validation conclusions.
Contribute to improvements in data handling, validation tooling and repeatable analytical processes, while working closely with more experienced colleagues on methodology and stakeholder communication.
Plan and execute independent validations of credit risk models and related data, assumptions and methodologies.
Assess model design, implementation, performance and limitations using appropriate quantitative and qualitative methods.
Analyse data quality, model behaviour and validation results, and convert that analysis into clear conclusions and recommendations.
Write validation documentation and present findings to relevant stakeholders, including senior management and regulatory counterparts where relevant.
Work with model developers, model owners and other stakeholders while maintaining an independent validation perspective.
Help improve the quality, efficiency and reproducibility of validation work.
Contribute to more standardised ways of working across code, data, analysis and reporting.


About you:


Relevant experience (2+ years) in model validation, model development, data science, quantitative analytics or similar fields.
The ability to work directly with data and analytical tooling in a controlled and structured way.
A university degree in a quantitative field such as mathematics, statistics, economics, engineering, physics or a similar discipline.
Motivation to develop deeper expertise in credit risk model validation where this has not been the candidate's main background to date.
Strong analytical skills and the ability to structure complex problems in a practical and rigorous way.
Experience working with quantitative analysis in Python and SQL as well as version control.
Experience with Databricks and modern development practices is an advantage.
Good judgement in working with data, including awareness of data quality, limitations and traceability.
Strong written and spoken English and the ability to explain technical matters clearly to different stakeholders.
A collaborative working style and a consistent focus on quality, accountability and timely delivery.


We offer:

If the position is located in Lithuania the monthly salary range will be from 3280 EUR to 4920 EUR gross (based on your competencies relevant for the job).

Additionally, each Danske Bank employee receives employee benefits package (benefits are only valid for Lithuania the same as the salary ranges) which includes:


Growth opportunities: professional & supportive team, e-learnings, numerous development programs; (incl. professional certificates); 100+ professions for internal mobility opportunities.
Health & Well-being: a diverse, inclusive, work & life balance work environment; additional health insurance; mental well-being practices; partial psychologist counselling compensation; silence and sleep zones at the office; game rooms.
Hybrid working conditions: home office budget (after the probation period); modern Danske Campus workplace developed with anthropologist for the best employee experience.
Additional days of leave: for rest, health, volunteering, exams in higher education institutions, and other important activities. Moreover, for seniority with Danske Bank.
Monetary compensation package: accidents & critical diseases insurance; financial support in case of unfortunate events, travel insurance; IIIrd Pillar Pension Fund contribution.


See all the benefits HERE.

Your title in job contract will be Risk Methodology & Model Validation.

If you're interested in this role and joining my team, feel free to contact me –Stallone Nobert, Head of Credit Risk Model Validation 1 via LinkedIn, and I will answer your questions!

Title: AI Engineer - Onsite Copenhagen
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
AI Engineer

AI is moving from prototypes to production-ready solutions.

The engineers who can take it there matter more than ever.

At Heyra, we build AI systems, workflows and data platforms that are meant to live in production from day one. We work closely with companies that are moving quickly and need solutions that hold up under real conditions.

We're looking for an AI Engineer who is as comfortable in the code as in the architecture. Someone who can design, build and ship AI solutions today, and gradually take ownership of how we approach AI architecture and strategy as the company grows.

You'll work closely with engineers, data specialists, strategists and designers in a company where ideas move quickly and the distance from thinking to building is intentionally small.

What You'll Work On


Designing and deploying AI solutions across domains like finance, operations and supply chain
Building predictive models, automated workflows and agentic systems
Leading client projects from problem to production
Translating business needs into AI solutions that actually ship
Integrating scalable AI systems through APIs and cloud environments
Mentoring teammates and shaping how Heyra approaches AI


You Probably Have Experience With


Software development, ML or data engineering, with AI applications running in production
AI and GenAI frameworks, ideally with some MLOps and evals exposure
Building scalable systems in cloud environments like Azure or GCP
Coding with agents such as Claude Code, Codex or Cursor
Stakeholder management and leading delivery end to end


Curiosity, taste and builder mentality matter just as much to us as specific tools or years of experience.

Why Heyra

At Heyra, we believe consulting needs to be rewritten. We're building a consultancy designed for the agentic era. We design, build, and operate AI products that change how enterprises work - from problem to prototype to production.

We care deeply about craft, speed and building things that work in the real world.

Heyra brings together people from engineering, strategy, product, startup and creative environments. We keep teams small, collaboration close and momentum high.

You'll Join a Team Where


people care deeply about what they build
good ideas move quickly
new technology gets explored early
and where there is room to shape both the company and the way we work


We're around 45 people today after merging with Pyne, and we're only just getting started.

If shaping how AI gets built and shipped at Heyra sounds like your kind of work, send us a short note about yourself together with your CV. We care much more about curiosity, energy and the way you think than polished applications.

Title: Data engineer
Place: Herlev, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About The Job

You will be joining a growing AI team with 10 other members and a core mission to turn data into measurable business value. Your primary role is to build and operate reliable data pipelines in a young scaleup on track for 300% YoY growth.

Your work will support projects ranging from AI voice agents and smart scheduling systems to large-scale data products serving 100,000+ merchants.

What You'll Do


Design, build, and own batch and streaming pipelines in Databricks (PySpark & Lakebase).
Model data for analytics and ML; maintain clean, well-documented tables in Unity Catalog.
Work with external APIs and internal teams to integrate new data sources
Implement data quality, lineage, and observability.
Optimize cost and performance
Partner with data scientists and product teams to deliver features.
Automate deployment and testing; keep pipelines simple and maintainable using Databricks workflows.


Who You Are


3+ years of experience as a data engineer.
Experience building production-grade data pipelines with PySpark and SQL.
Pragmatic and flexible mindset. You apply best practices when they help, bend them when they don’t.
Solid understanding of ETL/ELT design, data modelling, and partitioning strategies.
Databricks experience or equivalent
AWS familiarity (S3, IAM)


Why Flatpay?


Impact: Ship data solutions creating real impact in one of Europe’s fastest growing companies.
Meaningful products: Build pipelines and data infrastructure that support merchants and colleagues make better decisions, act on insights, and improve workflows.
Competitive salary
Vacation: 6 weeks paid
Relaxed atmosphere with (optional) social events such as minigolf evenings, and periodic hackathons & company parties in exciting off-site locations.
Flat structure and a strong action-over-process culture
Co-workers with a passion for data engineering and AI, and a passion for sharing knowledge daily.
Pension and health insurance, as well as almost unlimited company merch


About Us

Title: Data Analytics Lead
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
The Opportunity

We are looking for a Data Analytics Lead to join the Asset Strategy Platforms team in Copenhagen. In this role, you will own analytical workstreams end-to-end — from scoping and data modelling through to communicating findings and driving stakeholder action.

You will work with large, complex datasets spanning customer orders, supplier transactions, asset utilisation, and operational data. You will contribute to KPI frameworks, experiment design, and AI/ML use case development — using AI tools where they genuinely accelerate work, not as a default.

This role is suited to someone who is comfortable operating in a complex, data-rich environment, and who can translate insights into tangible business outcomes.

What You Will Do

Analytics & Insight

Identify and quantify opportunities in platform data; translate findings into clear recommendations for product and business teams
Design, build, and maintain decision-oriented KPI frameworks accessible to both front-line teams and senior management
Conduct deep-dive analyses on product adoption, user behaviour, performance trends, and business impact
Design and evaluate experiments using rigorous statistical methods
Support benefit estimation and validation for platform product initiatives

Data Engineering & Infrastructure

Write efficient SQL and Python to extract, transform, and model data
Build and maintain scalable data pipelines and reusable datasets
Work within Azure/Databricks infrastructure to ensure data quality and performance
Treat analytical datasets as data products with ownership, SLAs, and documentation
Identify and resolve data quality issues

Stakeholder Engagement & Delivery

Collaborate with product owners, process owners, and engineering teams
Communicate findings through data storytelling and actionable insights
Manage your analytical backlog and prioritisation
Proactively flag bias, fairness risks, or unintended consequences

AI/ML & GenAI

Build feature engineering pipelines and evaluation frameworks
Use AI tools (Copilot, Claude, ChatGPT) to accelerate workflows
Collaborate with data scientists and ML engineers

Deterministic vs non-deterministic outputs

You are expected to understand when deterministic outputs are required (e.g. KPIs, reporting) versus when probabilistic outputs are acceptable, and design appropriate validation, guardrails, and human-in-the-loop processes.

What We’re Looking For

Experience

Min 5-8 years in business analytics or data analysis, ideally in complex, large-scale environments
Proven delivery of end-to-end analytical projects
Experience working within or alongside product/platform teams
Hands-on use of AI tools in analytical workflows is a strong advantage

Technical Skills

Strong SQL and Python (pandas, numpy)
Experience with Azure and Databricks
Power BI or equivalent dashboarding tools
Knowledge of data pipelines, modelling, and data quality practices
Working knowledge of AI/ML concepts and model evaluation
Practical use of AI tools with strong validation judgement

Working Here

This role is based in Copenhagen within the Asset Strategy Platforms team, working closely with Product, Engineering, and Business stakeholders.

You will operate in a high-impact, data-driven environment where analytics directly influences platform decisions and operational outcomes.

Maersk is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace and supports individuals throughout the recruitment process.

Title: Dataanalytisk udvikler / BI-udvikler til Travelmind Group
Description:
Vil du være med til at gøre data til en aktiv del af forretningen – ikke kun i rapporter, men direkte i de systemer, beslutninger og processer, vi arbejder med hver dag?




Travelmind Group søger en dataanalytisk udvikler, der kan hjælpe os med at udnytte de store mængder data, vi allerede har på tværs af vores rejse- og feriebrands.




Vi driver blandt andet Campaya Sommerhusudlejning, skiferie.dk, Elcano Travel, Caddie Golfrejser og flere andre rejsebrands på fælles, egenudviklede platforme. Forretningen er i kraftig vækst, og vi arbejder målrettet med at blive endnu bedre til at bruge data til at skabe bedre kundeoplevelser, højere konvertering, bedre prissætning og mere effektive interne arbejdsgange.

Vi søger derfor en profil, der både kan forstå data, forretning og udvikling – og som kan være med til at gøre data langt mere tilgængelig og anvendelig i hele organisationen.




Rollen

Du bliver en del af vores udviklingsteam og kommer til at arbejde tæt sammen med både ledelse, salg, marketing, kundeservice og produktudvikling.




Din opgave bliver at hjælpe os med at gøre data mere anvendelig i hele forretningen. Ikke kun som rapporter og dashboards, men også som en integreret del af vores egne systemer, hvor data kan bruges til at skabe bedre beslutninger, bedre kundeoplevelser og mere intelligente digitale løsninger.




Vi har allerede store mængder data om blandt andet bookinger, priser, søgninger, produkter, kunder, husejere, salgskanaler og performance. Vi ønsker at blive langt bedre til at omsætte disse data til konkrete indsigter, automatiseringer og værktøjer, der kan bruges i hverdagen.




Det kan eksempelvis være data, der hjælper os med at forstå, hvorfor nogle ferieboliger performer bedre end andre, hvordan priser kan optimeres, hvordan søgninger og anbefalinger kan forbedres, eller hvor der er potentiale for vækst og effektivisering.




Dine opgaver kan blandt andet blive

Opbygning af rapporter og dashboards til ledelse, salg, marketing og drift
Udvikling af datagrundlag, datamodeller og integrationer på tværs af vores platforme
Analyse af bookingdata, konvertering, priser, belægning og kundeadfærd
Udvikling af modeller og værktøjer til prissætningsforslag, anbefalinger og optimering
Forbedring af søgning, sortering og produktvisning baseret på data
Segmentering af kunder, husejere og produkter
Automatisering af manuelle analyser og interne processer
Samarbejde med udviklere om at integrere dataindsigter direkte i vores platform
Eksperimenter med machine learning og AI-modeller, hvor det kan give reel forretningsmæssig værdi




Vi forestiller os, at du




Har en stærk analytisk tilgang og trives med at kombinere teknik, data og forretning.




Du behøver ikke passe præcist ind i én bestemt titel. Din baggrund kan eksempelvis være som BI-udvikler, data engineer, analytisk softwareudvikler, data analyst med stærke tekniske kompetencer eller lignende.




Det er en fordel, hvis du har erfaring med flere af følgende områder:

SQL og relationelle databaser, gerne SQL Server
Datamodellering, rapportering og BI
Power BI, Data Studio eller lignende værktøjer
Python eller R til analyse, databehandling eller modellering
C# og .NET-udvikling
Arbejde med API’er og integrationer
Analyse af kommercielle data, fx salg, priser, konvertering eller kundeadfærd
Machine learning, AI-modeller eller predictive analytics
At omsætte forretningsbehov til konkrete tekniske løsninger




Det vigtigste er, at du kan skabe bro mellem data og praktisk anvendelse. Vi leder efter en person, der både kan dykke ned i komplekse datasæt og samtidig forstå, hvordan resultaterne kan bruges i virkeligheden.




Om os




Travelmind Group er en dansk rejsekoncern med en række specialiserede rejsebrands, der alle bygger på fælles, egenudviklede teknologiske platforme.




Vores systemer håndterer store mængder produkter, priser, tilgængelighed, kunder, bookinger og marketingdata. Vi arbejder med online salg, automatisering, API-integrationer, SEO, performance marketing og løbende optimering af vores digitale platforme.




Vi har kontorer i blandt andet Horsens, Glostrup, Stockholm og Oslo.




Vi tilbyder

En central rolle i udviklingen af vores datadrevne arbejde
Mulighed for at påvirke både strategi, systemer og konkrete forretningsbeslutninger
Et udviklingsmiljø med korte beslutningsveje og stor praktisk indflydelse
Arbejde med data, der har direkte kommerciel betydning
Mulighed for at kombinere BI, softwareudvikling, data engineering og AI
Et uformelt miljø med dygtige kolleger og højt tempo
En virksomhed i vækst, hvor der er mange uløste og spændende dataopgaver




Hvem er du?




Du er nysgerrig, analytisk og teknisk stærk. Du motiveres af at finde mønstre, bygge løsninger og gøre komplekse data forståelige og anvendelige.




Du behøver ikke være specialist i alt fra dag ét, men du skal have lyst til at arbejde i krydsfeltet mellem softwareudvikling, dataanalyse, BI og forretning.




Du trives med at tage ansvar, stille spørgsmål og finde frem til de løsninger, der faktisk skaber værdi – ikke kun teknisk, men også kommercielt.




Ansøgning

Lyder det som en spændende udfordring, hører vi meget gerne fra dig.

Send din ansøgning og CV til:

cp@travelmind.group

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About Weld




The next generation of data infrastructure will be built for AI agents. Weld is built to be operated programmatically through APIs and MCP, so developers and AI agents can build and run the data foundations that modern companies depend on. We move data at scale across hundreds of integrations and increasingly serve as the backbone for agentic data workflows.




You can try Weld at weld.app.




The role




Most of your time is with customers. You onboard new customers, design their data setups on Weld with them, model the data that lands in their warehouse so it is ready for analytics and for agents to operate on, and unblock them when things break. You join pre-sales calls when prospects want to talk to someone technical, and those conversations focus on making their data usable by AI agents and on plugging Weld into their agentic workflows. When customers go deeper, designing transformation layers, structuring data for agent consumption, or building agent-driven systems on top of their warehouse, you consult with them and build alongside them. You do this work using the tools that make modern engineering fast: Claude Code for writing and refactoring, the Anthropic API for the agents and internal tooling that scale your work, and Weld itself, which you will operate through APIs and agents, the way our customers do. What you learn from customers shapes the product. You have a direct line to the founders and the engineering team, and your field insights influence what we build next, especially in how Weld exposes itself to agents through APIs, MCP, and tool interfaces.




What you will work on




Onboard new customers and design their data setups on Weld, from pipelines to warehouse models
Join pre-sales calls and technical evaluations, help prospects understand how Weld fits their stack, and shape early implementations
Diagnose pipeline issues end to end across our integrations and customer warehouses, and unblock customers fast
Help customers model their warehouse data, so it is clean, reliable, and ready for analytics or agent consumption
Consult with customers building agentic systems on top of Weld, helping them design transformation layers and structure data for agent consumption
Ship internal agents and tooling with Claude Code and the Anthropic API to scale how we onboard, support, and serve customers
Turn recurring customer problems into product improvements, and bring field insights back to the team to shape how Weld serves agentic workflows




We are looking for someone who




Has a solid understanding of modern data infrastructure, including ETL, reverse ETL, and data warehousing
Has experience with SQL and data modeling
Has built systems that interact with APIs programmatically
Has shipped something to production with LLMs or agents
Uses Claude Code, Cursor, or similar AI dev tools every day, and has opinions about them
Understands how modern agents are built: tool use, sub-agents, skills, MCP, and structured outputs
Has thought seriously about context engineering and how to evaluate agent behavior in production
Writes Python comfortably when needed
Communicates clearly and enjoys working directly with customers
Experience with TypeScript is a plus
A background in a customer-facing technical role such as professional services, solutions engineering, forward-deployed engineering, or analytics consulting is a plus




How to apply




Apply via LinkedIn with a short note. Include:

• A link or short write-up of something you have built with LLMs or agents. What it does, why you built it the way you did, and what you would change if you started over.

• A sentence or two on how you use AI tools in your own workflow today.




Working at Weld




Working at Weld means real ownership of what you work on, the freedom to make decisions, and a short distance between your work and what customers see. The role is based in our office in central Copenhagen, next to the Opera. We offer a competitive salary and equity, company off-sites, 6 weeks paid holiday, company-paid pension and health insurance, a MacBook Pro, and a company card from day one to buy what you need.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
KAYAK/momondo, part of Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG), is a leading travel search engine. With billions of queries across our platforms, we help people find their perfect flight, stay, rental car and vacation package. We’re also transforming business travel with a new corporate travel solution, KAYAK for Business.

As an employee of KAYAK, you will be part of a travel company that operates a portfolio of global metasearch brands including momondo, Cheapflights and HotelsCombined, among others. From start-up to industry leader, innovation is in our DNA and every employee has an opportunity to make their mark. Our focus is on building the best travel search engine to make it easier for everyone to experience the world.

KAYAK’s Organic Growth team, within Marketing SEO, uses travel search data to create timely, data-driven stories for media and public relations. We’re looking for a Data Analyst who can turn raw search signals into clear, reusable datasets, automated pipelines, and easy-to-use dashboards that support global PR and editorial campaigns.

This role will be required to work from our Copenhagen office at least 3 days per week.

Validate data quality and collaborate with Data Engineering to improve underlying models and delivery.
Document methods and make outputs easy for non-technical audiences to use.


Please Apply If You Have


A Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Statistics, Data Science, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field.
2-4 years of experience in a data analyst, business analyst, marketing analyst, or similar role.
Practical experience working with SQL and extracting/manipulating large datasets.
Hands-on experience in Python (or similar) for data processing and automation.
Familiarity with visualization tools (Superset, Datawrapper, Tableau, or similar).
Strong analytical thinking and the ability to translate complex data into clear, media-ready insights.
Comfortable explaining technical decisions to non-technical partners and delivering work on tight timelines.


Nice-to-have


Experience building reusable pipelines or automation for recurring reports.

Experience building lightweight self-serve dashboards.

Work from (almost) anywhere for up to 20 days per year

Title: Data Engineer - AI Applications
Description:
Join our innovative team at BESTSELLER as we leverage cutting-edge AI and data-driven solutions to transform the retail and e-commerce experience. We are looking for a Data Engineer who is passionate about building and operating the data products and platforms that power data-driven decisions, and eager to grow their craft in a fast-moving environment.

This role places you at the heart of our Appretio platform, our commitment to revolutionizing merchandising through data-driven solutions.

YOUR TEAM

You will become part of Merchandising Solutions, a department of more than 20 data, AI and technology specialists working to transform how merchandising decisions are made across BESTSELLER.

You will join a product team of ten highly skilled colleagues, including data scientists, engineers and application developers. Together, we build solutions that help our business make better decisions through data and AI. We work closely, share knowledge openly and value curiosity, ownership and continuous learning.

YOUR NEW ROLE

As a Data Engineer, you will help build and evolve the robust, scalable data pipelines and cloud-based infrastructure that power Appretio. Working closely with merchandisers, data scientists, engineers, and application developers, you will contribute to a data platform that is efficient, reliable, and ready to support cutting-edge AI initiatives. You will learn and apply data engineering best practices, take ownership of well-defined areas, and gradually grow into more senior responsibilities over time.

You will be supported through hands-on mentorship from experienced engineers, clear ownership of key areas, and ample opportunities to develop your skills and expertise.

Your Benefits

Joining BESTSELLER means becoming part of a collaborative and ambitious environment where technology creates tangible business impact.

You can expect:


The opportunity to build solutions used across multiple international fashion brands
Close collaboration with experienced engineers and data specialists
A culture focused on learning, knowledge sharing and continuous improvement
Professional development opportunities, including training, certification and new responsibilities as you grow
The freedom and responsibility to influence how we build our data platforms and products


YOUR PROFILE

You are proactive, structured and motivated by building reliable data solutions. You enjoy solving technical challenges, learning from others and continuously improving your craft. At the same time, you understand the value of collaboration and can communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

We imagine that you:


Hold a degree in Computer Science, Engineering or a related field, or bring equivalent practical experience
Have hands-on experience within data engineering and exposure to production data platforms
Have working knowledge of cloud environments such as AWS or Azure, data integration tools and modern data warehouse or lakehouse architectures
Possess solid SQL and Python skills and experience building data pipelines
Have experience with Git and CI/CD practices
Are interested in streaming and real-time data technologies such as Kafka or Spark Streaming
Are eager to learn, take responsibility and continue developing your technical expertise


It is considered an advantage if you also have:


An understanding of the trade-offs between transactional, analytical and distributed databases
Experience with multi-tenant database environments
Experience or interest in FastAPI, ORMs and related backend technologies
Experience optimising databases and pipelines for analytics-driven applications
An interest in data contracts, data products and data governance


WE ARE LOOKING FORWARD TO HEARING FROM YOU

If you have any questions about the position, please feel free to contact Director, Data & Tech Accelerator, Emil Hartvig Diekema, at +45 29 62 82 03. Please upload your CV as soon as possible and no later than 28 June 2026. We review applications on an ongoing basis and reserve the right to close the posting once we have found the right match.

Location: Your primary workplace will be at our office in Aarhus.

About BESTSELLER E-Commerce

At BESTSELLER, we are more than 15,000 people working for over 20 fashion brands such as JACK & JONES, SELECTED, VERO MODA, ONLY, PIECES, VILA, OBJECT, MAMALICIOUS, NAME IT, NOISY MAY and Y.A.S. At BESTSELLER E-Commerce, we are an international team of 170 people from more than 40 nationalities with the purpose to drive all e-commerce functions at BESTSELLER. With our office in the heart of Amsterdam as well as locations in Poland and Denmark, we are supporting all fashion brands of the BESTSELLER family and sell online in 15 countries around the world. We work in an environment where fashion meets technology. Challenging ourselves to offer the best experience to our customers and stakeholders and to work with the latest technologies is what drives us. As a team, we value autonomy and flexibility. We thrive in a flat organisation, where everyone has the chance to grow and learn. For more information, please go to www.bestseller.com.

Title: Forward Deployed AI Engineer, Denmark - BCG X
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.

We Are BCG X

We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions.

What You'll Do

Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions.

Our AI Software Engineers and Senior AI Software Engineers are part of our rapidly growing engineering team and help to build the next generation of AI solutions. You’ll have the chance to partner with clients in a variety of BCG regions and industries, and on key topics like climate change, enabling them to design, build, and deploy new and innovative solutions. Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X.

We Are Looking For Dedicated Individuals With a Passion For Software Development, Large-scale Data Analytics And Redefining Organizations Into AI Led Innovative Companies. Successful Candidates Possess The Following


Apply software development practices and standards to develop robust and maintainable software
Actively involved in every part of the software development process
Experienced at guiding non-technical teams and consultants in and best practices for robust software development
Optimize and enhance computational efficiency of algorithms and software design
Motivated by a fast-paced, service-oriented environment and interacting directly with clients on new features for future product releases
Enjoy collaborating in teams to share software design and solution ideas
A natural problem-solver and intellectually curious across a breadth of industries and topics


What You'll Bring

Technologies

Programming Languages: Python

#BCGXjob

Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.

BCG is an E - Verify Employer. Click here for more information on E-Verify.

Title: ID Power Algo Trader
Description:
Description Summary




ID Power Algo Trader – AAL/CPH

ROLE OVERVIEW

We are currently looking for a technically strong, analytically rigorous trader with deep experience in continuous markets and real-time modelling. This pivotal role calls for a seasoned professional who combines strong trading intuition with advanced statistical and programming capabilities, and who can translate ambiguous problems into structured models that drive PnL.

As a key member of our team, you will have the opportunity to contribute directly to the performance and evolution of our intraday power trading activities. This position requires analytical depth, strong decision-making under uncertainty, and a deep understanding of market structure, pricing mechanics, and liquidity dynamics. You must be comfortable operating in real-time environments, adapting to shifting market conditions, and continuously improving models and systems to enhance predictive accuracy and trading outcomes. Collaboration is central to success in this role; therefore, the ability to work seamlessly across trading and engineering teams is essential to optimizing strategies, improving infrastructure, and scaling automated decision-making.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Design and develop real-time fair value models for intraday power markets, from initial Python prototypes through to production in close collaboration with engineering.
Build, curate, and maintain high-quality datasets by integrating fundamental and real-time market data, including assessing sourcing strategies and data requirements for accurate forecasting.
Design and implement advanced predictive models to forecast energy market dynamics and inform trading strategy.
Adjust model and system parameters in response to shifting market conditions, liquidity regimes, or evolving trading situations.
Monitor, validate, and iterate on production models to ensure strong performance, improving predictive accuracy and trading outcomes over time.
Identify and execute market-take opportunities driven by model insights and power market structure.
Identify edge cases, structural risks, and liquidity gaps, proactively addressing them and documenting findings for research and system improvement.
Collaborate cross-functionally with engineering to optimize strategies, enhance infrastructure, and scale automated decision-making.

QUALIFICATIONS AND REQUIREMENTS

Bachelor's degree in Mathematics, Economics, Engineering, or a related field.
Possession of a master’s degree or professional certifications will be considered an advantage.
A minimum of 3 years' experience is required
Strong understanding of trading concepts, decision science, and probabilistic reasoning.
Extensive experience with continuous trading, whether in prediction markets, betting, crypto, or traditional markets, with specific expertise in market structure, pricing mechanics, and liquidity dynamics.
Proficiency in Python and SQL, with deep working knowledge of the data and machine learning ecosystem.
Strong skills in building and managing complex datasets, including sourcing, cleaning, and structuring data for analysis.
Expertise in predictive modelling, with a track record of building accurate forecasting tools for continuous markets.
Ability to think critically about uncertainty, model assumptions, and real-world edge cases.
Fluency in English is required. Knowledge of additional languages will be viewed favourably.

WHAT WE OFFER

Competitive compensation, inclusive of performance-based bonuses.
Comprehensive benefits package.
Pathways for professional growth and career development.
A dynamic and supportive work environment where innovation and creativity are encouraged.
Opportunity to be part of building a great business where your efforts are recognized and valued.

Thank you for your interest in Balyasny. Please understand that we have a large volume of applicants and regrettably cannot reply to each one, if your profile is suitable we will reach out.
