Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Spirii develops world-class charging products and digital solutions for electric vehicles. Our Digital Development department fosters a listen, learn, and lead culture — allowing us to continuously improve how we work. We're looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to build and evolve Spirii's data platform and pipeline infrastructure. Working closely with our Data Lead and domain teams, you'll design and maintain reliable ELT pipelines, ensure data arrives in Amazon Redshift correctly and on time, and support the analytics layer that powers insights across EV charging events, subscriptions, and financial transactions. This is a unique opportunity to shape Spirii's data infrastructure from an early stage — establishing standards for ingestion, orchestration, and data reliability as we scale.

Your Responsibilities

Pipeline & Ingestion


Design, build, and maintain scalable ELT pipelines that bring data from product backends, third-party services, and operational systems into Amazon Redshift.
Own ingestion reliability: monitoring, alerting, and incident response for pipeline failures.
Evaluate and improve orchestration tooling and scheduling.


Data Platform & Infrastructure


Evolve Spirii's data platform architecture: storage, compute, access control, and cost optimisation in Redshift.
Implement and maintain infrastructure-as-code practices for data workflows (Terraform/CDK or similar).
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data infrastructure and transformations.


Data Quality & Governance


Implement data quality checks, freshness monitoring, and anomaly detection across ingestion and transformation layers.
Work with Analytics Engineers and domain teams to define and enforce data contracts and schema standards.
Ensure GDPR-compliant data handling: PII identification, access control, and data retention policies.


Collaboration & Stakeholders


Partner with Engineering and Product teams to instrument events correctly at the source — schemas, identifiers, and change management.
Collaborate with Data team to ensure the analytics layer is built on reliable, well-modelled foundations.
Contribute to a strong data culture by creating and enhancing documentation, runbooks, data lineage, discoverability and incident response capabilities.


About You


You care deeply about data reliability and building pipelines that teams can depend on.
You're hands-on: you build, test, and ship pipelines and infrastructure improvements yourself.
You think of data pipelines as products — you care about uptime, observability, and the downstream consumers who depend on them.
You're pragmatic: you know when to engineer robustly and when to keep things lean.
You collaborate well across teams and communicate clearly about trade-offs and status.


Qualifications


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
3+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role with a strong data focus.
Strong SQL skills and solid experience building production data pipelines.
Hands-on experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred; BigQuery/Snowflake/Databricks also relevant).
Experience with orchestration tools (Apache Airflow or similar).
Proficiency in Python for data work (pipeline scripts, transformations, tooling).
Solid understanding of ELT/ETL concepts, including incremental loading, CDC, and idempotency.
Familiar with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CDK, or similar).
Familiar with data governance concepts: access control, PII handling, GDPR-minded practices.


Nice to Have


Experience with data observability tooling.
Experience with event/data contract patterns in product analytics (schemas, versioning, instrumentation).
Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
Experience with dbt (understanding of transformation layer and how it interfaces with pipelines).
Machine learning infrastructure experience is a plus, but not required for this role.


Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work-life balance.


Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.
Competitive Package: A competitive salary, an 11% company pension plan, and a comprehensive benefits package.
Time to Recharge: Benefit from 32 paid vacation days (5 weeks + 5 extra) and paid parental leave to focus on what matters most.
Career Growth: Access professional development opportunities and grow your career in a high-impact, high-growth industry.
Stay Energized: Enjoy excellent coffee, tea, free snacks, and subsidized lunches at the office.
Stay Active: Use our Spirii gym and shared company car to keep moving.
Make an Impact: Be part of a team driving the future of sustainable energy while working on meaningful projects.
Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.


The position is a permanent position in Copenhagen, Denmark. Danish language is not a requirement, but fluent English language skills are required.

About Spirii – Who We Are

Spirii is a full-service e-Mobility provider, which offers hardware and software charging solutions to companies in the private and the public sector. Spirii has recently celebrated its six-year anniversary as a founded company; however the people in Spirii have extensive experience working with electric cars and charging, as well as the development of future transport and energy systems.

Spirii is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences. Spirii strives to create an inclusive and diverse workplace, and we encourage all qualified applicants to apply regardless of age, gender, race, sexual/religious orientation, or disability.

HTML:
Spirii develops world-class charging products and digital solutions for electric vehicles. Our Digital Development department fosters a listen, learn, and lead culture — allowing us to continuously improve how we work. We're looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to build and evolve Spirii's data platform and pipeline infrastructure. Working closely with our Data Lead and domain teams, you'll design and maintain reliable ELT pipelines, ensure data arrives in Amazon Redshift correctly and on time, and support the analytics layer that powers insights across EV charging events, subscriptions, and financial transactions. This is a unique opportunity to shape Spirii's data infrastructure from an early stage — establishing standards for ingestion, orchestration, and data reliability as we scale.

Your Responsibilities

Pipeline & Ingestion

  • Design, build, and maintain scalable ELT pipelines that bring data from product backends, third-party services, and operational systems into Amazon Redshift.
  • Own ingestion reliability: monitoring, alerting, and incident response for pipeline failures.
  • Evaluate and improve orchestration tooling and scheduling.

Data Platform & Infrastructure

  • Evolve Spirii's data platform architecture: storage, compute, access control, and cost optimisation in Redshift.
  • Implement and maintain infrastructure-as-code practices for data workflows (Terraform/CDK or similar).
  • Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data infrastructure and transformations.

Data Quality & Governance

  • Implement data quality checks, freshness monitoring, and anomaly detection across ingestion and transformation layers.
  • Work with Analytics Engineers and domain teams to define and enforce data contracts and schema standards.
  • Ensure GDPR-compliant data handling: PII identification, access control, and data retention policies.

Collaboration & Stakeholders

  • Partner with Engineering and Product teams to instrument events correctly at the source — schemas, identifiers, and change management.
  • Collaborate with Data team to ensure the analytics layer is built on reliable, well-modelled foundations.
  • Contribute to a strong data culture by creating and enhancing documentation, runbooks, data lineage, discoverability and incident response capabilities.

About You

  • You care deeply about data reliability and building pipelines that teams can depend on.
  • You're hands-on: you build, test, and ship pipelines and infrastructure improvements yourself.
  • You think of data pipelines as products — you care about uptime, observability, and the downstream consumers who depend on them.
  • You're pragmatic: you know when to engineer robustly and when to keep things lean.
  • You collaborate well across teams and communicate clearly about trade-offs and status.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
  • 3+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role with a strong data focus.
  • Strong SQL skills and solid experience building production data pipelines.
  • Hands-on experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred; BigQuery/Snowflake/Databricks also relevant).
  • Experience with orchestration tools (Apache Airflow or similar).
  • Proficiency in Python for data work (pipeline scripts, transformations, tooling).
  • Solid understanding of ELT/ETL concepts, including incremental loading, CDC, and idempotency.
  • Familiar with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CDK, or similar).
  • Familiar with data governance concepts: access control, PII handling, GDPR-minded practices.

Nice to Have

  • Experience with data observability tooling.
  • Experience with event/data contract patterns in product analytics (schemas, versioning, instrumentation).
  • Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
  • Experience with dbt (understanding of transformation layer and how it interfaces with pipelines).
  • Machine learning infrastructure experience is a plus, but not required for this role.

Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work-life balance.

  • Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.
  • Competitive Package: A competitive salary, an 11% company pension plan, and a comprehensive benefits package.
  • Time to Recharge: Benefit from 32 paid vacation days (5 weeks + 5 extra) and paid parental leave to focus on what matters most.
  • Career Growth: Access professional development opportunities and grow your career in a high-impact, high-growth industry.
  • Stay Energized: Enjoy excellent coffee, tea, free snacks, and subsidized lunches at the office.
  • Stay Active: Use our Spirii gym and shared company car to keep moving.
  • Make an Impact: Be part of a team driving the future of sustainable energy while working on meaningful projects.
  • Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.

The position is a permanent position in Copenhagen, Denmark. Danish language is not a requirement, but fluent English language skills are required.

Spirii is a full-service e-Mobility provider, which offers hardware and software charging solutions to companies in the private and the public sector. Title: Data engineer
Place: Herlev, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About The Job

You will be joining a growing AI team with 10 other members and a core mission to turn data into measurable business value. Your primary role is to build and operate reliable data pipelines in a young scaleup on track for 300% YoY growth.

Your work will support projects ranging from AI voice agents and smart scheduling systems to large-scale data products serving 100,000+ merchants.

What You'll Do


Design, build, and own batch and streaming pipelines in Databricks (PySpark & Lakebase).
Model data for analytics and ML; maintain clean, well-documented tables in Unity Catalog.
Work with external APIs and internal teams to integrate new data sources
Implement data quality, lineage, and observability.
Optimize cost and performance
Partner with data scientists and product teams to deliver features.
Automate deployment and testing; keep pipelines simple and maintainable using Databricks workflows.


Who You Are


3+ years of experience as a data engineer.
Experience building production-grade data pipelines with PySpark and SQL.
Pragmatic and flexible mindset. You apply best practices when they help, bend them when they don’t.
Solid understanding of ETL/ELT design, data modelling, and partitioning strategies.
Databricks experience or equivalent
AWS familiarity (S3, IAM)


Why Flatpay?


Impact: Ship data solutions creating real impact in one of Europe’s fastest growing companies.
Meaningful products: Build pipelines and data infrastructure that support merchants and colleagues make better decisions, act on insights, and improve workflows.
Competitive salary
Vacation: 6 weeks paid
Relaxed atmosphere with (optional) social events such as minigolf evenings, and periodic hackathons & company parties in exciting off-site locations.
Flat structure and a strong action-over-process culture
Co-workers with a passion for data engineering and AI, and a passion for sharing knowledge daily.
Pension and health insurance, as well as almost unlimited company merch


About Us

Welcome to Flatpay!

Flatpay is not just another payment solution; we are dedicated to innovating the market for payment solutions and financial services in the SME segment with our customer-centric and transparent services.

Today, we are one of the fastest-growing fintech companies out of the Nordics - and Denmark's fastest unicorn!

Flatpay is more than just work. We believe in fostering a vibrant and fun work culture where our ambitious colleagues can thrive. As part of our team, you will be one of +1.700 passionate colleagues dedicated to making a difference in the world of payments.

What sets Flatpay apart is our dedication. We continuously strive to stay ahead of the curve, adapting our solutions to meet our client's evolving needs.

Let's shape the future of merchants together!

Title: Senior data scientist GenAI
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Senior data scientist GenAI

Are you passionate about cutting-edge AI and language models? Do you want to shape the future of how personal customers interact with Danske Bank? We are looking for a Senior Data Scientist with deep expertise in Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Generative AI (GenAI) to join our Conversational AI unit within the Personal Customers AI Centre of Excellence.

Who Are We

The Personal Customers AI Centre of Excellence is a diverse team of data scientists, ML engineers and MLOps engineers. Our Conversational AI unit builds adviser and customer-facing GenAI-powered solutions and stays at the forefront of technology and research to create real value for customers.

What You Will Do

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will lead the development of language-driven solutions for our Conversational AI unit, enhancing the AI Assistant with actionable flows, building RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems connected to internal data and co-designing multi-agent sets that empower customers with self-service capabilities.

Your key responsibilities will include:


Designing, developing and optimising advanced NLP/GenAI solutions using LLMs, including auxiliary tools such as NER, offensive speech detection, information masking and summarisation on multi-turn conversational input.
Expanding and implementing RAG pipelines that integrate vector databases and embedding models for structured and unstructured data and making them work effectively in a conversational setting.
Collaborating with ML engineers, data engineers and product teams to integrate and deploy models into production environments using Python, core ML libraries, Docker, Kubernetes and cloud platforms (AWS Bedrock, Azure, GCP, Databricks).
Driving experimentation, A/B testing and model evaluation strategies using metrics such as BLEU, ROUGE, F1, exact match, faithfulness and hallucination detection, and continuously improving latency, accuracy and LLM judges.
Staying current with the latest research and industry trends in LLMs, transformers and prompt engineering, identifying areas for improvement in existing solutions, and mentoring junior team members while contributing to best practices in model development and MLOps.


Your Skills And Experience


5+ years of experience in data science or machine learning with a strong focus on NLP, and solid knowledge of transformers, tokenisation, embeddings and attention mechanisms.
Hands-on experience with GenAI frameworks and tooling such as LangChain, LlamaIndex, OpenAI APIs and Hugging Face, plus strong Python skills and familiarity with PyTorch, TensorFlow and scikit-learn.
Proven experience building and deploying RAG architectures and vector-based retrieval (FAISS, Pinecone, Weaviate, OpenSearch) in production, working with data and services in AWS and Databricks, and deploying models using Docker, Kubernetes and cloud platforms.
Strong understanding of evaluation metrics for NLP tasks and excellent problem-solving and communication skills, with the ability to explain complex topics to different stakeholders.


It is a plus if you have experience with prompt engineering and instruction-tuned models, fine-tuning domain-specific LLMs, responsible AI principles and model risk management, multi-agent orchestration frameworks (for example LangGraph or AWS Agent Core), and Databricks for processing and model serving. Experience from domains such as finance, healthcare or customer service chatbots is also beneficial.

Why join us?

You will work on cutting-edge GenAI projects with real-world impact for millions of customers in a department with a solid track record of scaled solutions. You will join a collaborative, cross-functional team with room for autonomy and a flexible working environment, and you will work in our new headquarters with great facilities.

Interested?

Apply now or reach out to Ledian Selimaj – LEDS@danskebank.dk for more information. First interviews will start in June, so do not hold back.

Title: Forward Deployed AI Scientist, Denmark - BCG X
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.

We Are BCG X

We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions.

What You'll Do

Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions.

As a Data Scientist and Senior Data Scientist, you'll be part of our rapidly growing team. You'll have the chance to apply data science methods and analytics to real-world business situations across a variety of industries to drive significant business impact. You'll have the chance to partner with clients in a variety of BCG regions and industries, and on key topics like climate change, enabling them to design, build, and deploy new and innovative solutions.

Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X. Successful candidates are intellectually curious builders who are biased toward action, scrappy, and communicative.

We Are Looking For Talented Individuals With a Passion For Data Science, Statistics, Operations Research And Transforming Organizations Into AI Led Innovative Companies. Successful Candidates Possess The Following


Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams
Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations
Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner
Love building things and are comfortable working with modern development tools and writing code collaboratively (bonus points if you have a software development or DevOps experience)
Significant experience applying advanced analytics to a variety of business situations and a proven ability to synthesize complex data
Deep understanding of modern machine learning techniques and their mathematical underpinnings, and can translate this into business implications for our clients
Have strong project management skills


What You'll Bring

Technologies

Programming Languages: Python

#BCGXjob

Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.

BCG is an E - Verify Employer. Click here for more information on E-Verify.

Title: AI Engineer
Description:
Location: Copenhagen

Reports to: Director of AI




The Opportunity

Dawn Health is a leading Digital Health product company that empowers Life Science companies to create elegantly designed digital solutions quickly and in compliance with the highest quality and reliability standards. We are looking for a AI/ML Engineer to join our team in Copenhagen.




You'll join our core platform team shipping features into live regulated products, working shoulder-to-shoulder with engineers, clinicians, product managers, and designers. If you want your work to reach real patients - this is the role.




Role Overview and Responsibilities

Design and ship AI-powered features - RAG pipelines, conversational interfaces, AI-assisted data interpretation.
Own end-to-end AI pipelines: prompt engineering, orchestration, output evaluation, and production monitoring.
Build and maintain data pipelines for structured and unstructured data; define data requirements as part of AI feature design.
Design evaluation frameworks and run automated benchmarks using standard metrics
Build AI systems with clear architecture - clean input/output contracts, REST APIs
Contribute to AI verification & validation (V&V) documentation and compliance activities within our ISO 13485 / SaMD compatible environment.
Identify and communicate AI/ML-related product and compliance risks; provide SME input to support decision-making.




Your Mindset

You are driven by purpose and genuinely motivated to improve patients' lives through technology.
You are AI-native - you actively experiment with new models, frameworks, and tooling to work faster and build better.
You explain technical complexity clearly to non-technical peers and write documentation others actually want to read.
You are a collaborative team player who shares knowledge freely and helps others grow.




What Success Looks Like (6-12 Months)

You have independently owned and shipped a RAG pipeline or LLM-powered feature that is actively used across one or more Dawn Health products.
Your observability and benchmarking setup gives the team real visibility into model performance in production, and you are driving iterative improvements based on the data.
Leveraging established Dawn Health templates and AI-augmented workflows to improve efficiency and impact.
Clients and cross-functional teams view you as a trusted, proactive partner.




About You

3-5 years of ML/AI engineering experience, with at least 1-2 years working with AI/ML systems in production.
Strong Python; hands-on with LLM APIs, prompt engineering, and RAG patterns. Familiarity with LangChain/LangGraph, LlamaIndex, or equivalent.
MLOps fluency: CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions / Azure Pipeline) and observability tools (e.g. LangFuse / LangSmith).
Solid ML fundamentals: supervised/unsupervised/RL, model evaluation, interpretability, and bias mitigation.
Familiar with regulated environments: EU AI Act, ISO 27001, OWASP GenAI Top 10, and pharma-specific processes.
Relevant Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, ML, or equivalent practical experience.
Bonus: fine-tuning open-source LLMs, digital biomarkers / PROs, AI agent frameworks, or medical device context.




What We Offer

A portfolio of meaningful products that impact patient lives at scale.
Autonomy, trust, and support to do the best work of your career.
An open, collaborative environment with talented peers across engineering, design, clinical, and regulatory.
Career growth in one of the leading digital health scale-ups, alongside world-class pharma partners.
An array of benefits within the areas of culture, professional development, and a competitive health package.
A social culture with running, book, winter bathing, knitting, and crossfit clubs to join
Aaaand, probably the best lunch in Copenhagen, and the office view to go with it ☕




About Dawn Health

Dawn Health is a digital health company specializing in developing Software as Medical Device (SaMD) and other regulated healthcare solutions. We deliver both strategic consulting and development of innovative digital therapies targeting patients on a global scale.

Working at Dawn Health is more than just a job. We are not just looking for new employees. We are looking for deeply passionate souls, gifted minds, innovators, team players, entrepreneurs and people who believe they can change the world.




We love to build things, change the status quo, and drive a new digital health agenda that will impact 1 million patients by 2030. Our passion not only includes the urge to make a difference and Code to Save Lives - we're also very focused on our people. You’ll find warm, helpful, and friendly colleagues who care about each other and the mission we’re on.

  • MLOps fluency: CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions / Azure Pipeline) and observability tools (e.g. LangFuse / LangSmith).
  • Solid ML fundamentals: supervised/unsupervised/RL, model evaluation, interpretability, and bias mitigation.
  • Familiar with regulated environments: EU AI Act, ISO 27001, OWASP GenAI Top 10, and pharma-specific processes.
  • Relevant Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, ML, or equivalent practical experience.
  • Bonus: fine-tuning open-source LLMs, digital biomarkers / PROs, AI agent frameworks, or medical device context.


What We Offer

  • A portfolio of meaningful products that impact patient lives at scale.
  • Autonomy, trust, and support to do the best work of your career.
  • An open, collaborative environment with talented peers across engineering, design, clinical, and regulatory.
  • Career growth in one of the leading digital health scale-ups, alongside world-class pharma partners.
  • An array of benefits within the areas of culture, professional development, and a competitive health package.
  • A social culture with running, book, winter bathing, knitting, and crossfit clubs to join
  • Aaaand, probably the best lunch in Copenhagen, and the office view to go with it ☕


About Dawn Health

Dawn Health is a digital health company specializing in developing Software as Medical Device (SaMD) and other regulated healthcare solutions. We deliver both strategic consulting and development of innovative digital therapies targeting patients on a global scale.

Working at Dawn Health is more than just a job. We are not just looking for new employees. We are looking for deeply passionate souls, gifted minds, innovators, team players, entrepreneurs and people who believe they can change the world.


We love to build things, change the status quo, and drive a new digital health agenda that will impact 1 million patients by 2030. Our passion not only includes the urge to make a difference and Code to Save Lives - we're also very focused on our people. You’ll find warm, helpful, and friendly colleagues who care about each other and the mission we’re on.

Title: Data & AI Engineer
Place: Esbjerg Municipality, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Data & AI Engineer




ESVAGT er en af de førende aktører inden for specialiserede offshore services til vind- og olie-/gasindustrien. Vores skibe opererer bredt i Europa, og koncernen er i fortsat udvikling med nye kontrakter, en voksende flåde og et stigende fokus på digitale løsninger og stærke processer. Data, automatisering og AI spiller en stadig vigtigere rolle i vores forretning, og vi søger derfor en Data & AI Engineer til vores Digitalization & AI-team i Esbjerg.

I rollen bliver du en del af arbejdet med at udvikle og vedligeholde data-, integrations-, automatiserings- og AI-løsninger på tværs af ESVAGT. Du vil arbejde tæt sammen med forretningen og eksterne samarbejdspartnere for at omsætte behov og idéer til praktiske løsninger, der skaber værdi i den daglige drift.

Digitalization & AI-teamet har ansvaret for at understøtte ESVAGT’s digitale udvikling gennem data, integrationer, automatisering og anvendelse af AI. Rollen er hands-on og vil blandt andet omfatte vedligeholdelse og videreudvikling af vores Data Warehouse, data pipelines, integrationer, AI-understøttede løsninger og på sigt løsninger baseret på vessel data, IoT og digital twin.




Rollen vil blandt andet omfatte:

Udvikle og vedligeholde data pipelines, integrationer og dataflows på tværs af ESVAGT’s digitale platforme.
Vedligeholde og videreudvikle ESVAGT’s Data Warehouse, herunder datamodeller, pipelines og dokumentation.
Understøtte integrationsprojekter mellem forretningssystemer, herunder API’er, dataudveksling og cloud-baserede services.
Udvikle og implementere AI-understøttede løsninger, eksempelvis AI-agenter, automatiseringsflows og værktøjer, der understøtter daglige arbejdsgange.
Samarbejde med forretningen om at forstå behov og omsætte dem til praktiske løsninger inden for data, automatisering og AI.
Bidrage til løsninger baseret på vessel data, IoT og operationelle data, herunder på sigt digital twin, performance monitoring og predictive insights.
Sikre dokumentation, datakvalitet, governance og best practice på tværs af data- og AI-løsninger.

 

Foretrukne kompetencer

Stillingen er nyoprettet i ESVAGT, og derfor kan der være flere indgangsvinkler til jobbet. Vi ser gerne kandidater med erfaring fra lignende roller, men er også åbne for andre tekniske profiler med det rette fundament og den rette motivation. Det er ikke afgørende, at du kan det hele fra første dag. Vi tilbyder relevant oplæring, kurser og kompetenceudvikling inden for de områder, hvor der er behov.

Erfaring med data engineering, softwareudvikling, integrationer, cloud-løsninger eller lignende tekniske områder.
Stærke SQL-kompetencer og erfaring med strukturerede data.
Kendskab til Microsoft Azure eller andre cloud-platforme er en fordel.
Erfaring med API’er, systemintegrationer, data pipelines eller automatiseringsflows er en fordel.
Interesse for AI-teknologier og for, hvordan de kan anvendes i en praktisk forretningskontekst.
Kendskab til Copilot Studio, Power Automate, Azure AI Foundry eller tilsvarende AI- og automatiseringsværktøjer er en fordel, men ikke et krav.
Evne til at forstå forretningsprocesser og omsætte behov til tekniske løsninger.
Struktureret tilgang til problemløsning, dokumentation, datakvalitet og sikkerhed.
Gode kommunikationsevner på dansk og engelsk.
Kendskab til IoT, vessel data, maritime operationer, digital twin eller ERP-/integrationslandskaber er ikke et krav, men vil være en fordel.

 

Derfor skal du vælge ESVAGT

Hos ESVAGT får du mulighed for at blive en del af et centralt team, hvor du kan være med til at præge udviklingen af en ny Digitalization & AI-funktion.

Du kommer til at arbejde med konkrete forretningsnære problemstillinger og løsninger, hvor data, integrationer, automatisering og AI skal skabe værdi i praksis. Rollen giver dig en bred berøringsflade på tværs af forretningen og mulighed for at se effekten af de løsninger, du er med til at skabe.




Vi tilbyder attraktive personalegoder, herunder sundhedsforsikring, tandforsikring, frokostordning og sociale arrangementer. Derudover har vi fokus på faglig udvikling og en sund balance mellem arbejde og fritid.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Martin Glinka, Head of Digitalization & AI, på

+45 78 730 726.




Kan du se dig selv i rollen som vores nye Data & AI Engineer, ser vi frem til at modtage din ansøgning via linket. Vi behandler ansøgninger løbende og afslutter processen, når den rette kandidat er fundet.

Title: Power & Gas Quantitative Analyst - Algo Research & Development
Description:
IMC is continuing to grow its trading initiative in Aarhus, Denmark, to become a major player in the European Power and Gas markets. This expansion is built on a strong foundation of experienced external hires in Trading and internal talent in Technology. With a collaborative and entrepreneurial culture, the team offers a unique opportunity to contribute to shaping and scaling this new business.

The group combines deep expertise with broad trading experience and values innovation, ownership and cross-functional collaboration. As this business continues to grow, there will be increasing opportunities to take on broader responsibilities and contribute meaningfully to its success.

Core Responsibilities


Generate and validate high-quality alpha signals using statistical methods and machine learning, with strong focus on robustness and overfitting
Combine market microstructure and energy fundamentals to build predictive trading signals
Build and maintain reliable, production-ready models with clear testing and validation standards


Skills And Experience


2+ years of experience developing quantitative models, ML-driven signals, or trading strategies in financial futures markets, preferably within energy
Strong foundation in probability and statistics, with substantial real-world experience applying machine learning or deep learning
Strong programming skills (Python preferred), with experience writing production-quality code
Experience working with large-scale time series data
Practical mindset focused on deployable solutions and real-world trading performance


About Us

IMC is a global trading firm powered by a cutting-edge research environment and a world-class technology backbone. Since 1989, we’ve been a stabilizing force in financial markets, providing essential liquidity upon which market participants depend. Across our offices in the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, and India, our talented quant researchers, engineers, traders, and business operations professionals are united by our uniquely collaborative, high-performance culture, and our commitment to giving back. From entering dynamic new markets to embracing disruptive technologies, and from developing an innovative research environment to diversifying our trading strategies, we dare to continuously innovate and collaborate to succeed.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Spirii develops world-class charging products and digital solutions for electric vehicles. Our Digital Development department fosters a listen, learn, and lead culture — allowing us to continuously improve how we work. We're looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to build and evolve Spirii's data platform and pipeline infrastructure. Working closely with our Data Lead and domain teams, you'll design and maintain reliable ELT pipelines, ensure data arrives in Amazon Redshift correctly and on time, and support the analytics layer that powers insights across EV charging events, subscriptions, and financial transactions. This is a unique opportunity to shape Spirii's data infrastructure from an early stage — establishing standards for ingestion, orchestration, and data reliability as we scale.

Your Responsibilities

Pipeline & Ingestion


Design, build, and maintain scalable ELT pipelines that bring data from product backends, third-party services, and operational systems into Amazon Redshift.
Own ingestion reliability: monitoring, alerting, and incident response for pipeline failures.
Evaluate and improve orchestration tooling and scheduling.


Data Platform & Infrastructure


Evolve Spirii's data platform architecture: storage, compute, access control, and cost optimisation in Redshift.
Implement and maintain infrastructure-as-code practices for data workflows (Terraform/CDK or similar).
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data infrastructure and transformations.


Data Quality & Governance


Implement data quality checks, freshness monitoring, and anomaly detection across ingestion and transformation layers.
Work with Analytics Engineers and domain teams to define and enforce data contracts and schema standards.
Ensure GDPR-compliant data handling: PII identification, access control, and data retention policies.


Collaboration & Stakeholders


Partner with Engineering and Product teams to instrument events correctly at the source — schemas, identifiers, and change management.
Collaborate with Data team to ensure the analytics layer is built on reliable, well-modelled foundations.
Contribute to a strong data culture by creating and enhancing documentation, runbooks, data lineage, discoverability and incident response capabilities.


About You


You care deeply about data reliability and building pipelines that teams can depend on.
You're hands-on: you build, test, and ship pipelines and infrastructure improvements yourself.
You think of data pipelines as products — you care about uptime, observability, and the downstream consumers who depend on them.
You're pragmatic: you know when to engineer robustly and when to keep things lean.
You collaborate well across teams and communicate clearly about trade-offs and status.


Qualifications


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
3+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role with a strong data focus.
Strong SQL skills and solid experience building production data pipelines.
Hands-on experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred; BigQuery/Snowflake/Databricks also relevant).
Experience with orchestration tools (Apache Airflow or similar).
Proficiency in Python for data work (pipeline scripts, transformations, tooling).
Solid understanding of ELT/ETL concepts, including incremental loading, CDC, and idempotency.
Familiar with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CDK, or similar).
Familiar with data governance concepts: access control, PII handling, GDPR-minded practices.


Nice to Have


Experience with data observability tooling.
Experience with event/data contract patterns in product analytics (schemas, versioning, instrumentation).
Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
Experience with dbt (understanding of transformation layer and how it interfaces with pipelines).
Machine learning infrastructure experience is a plus, but not required for this role.


Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work-life balance.


Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.
Competitive Package: A competitive salary, an 11% company pension plan, and a comprehensive benefits package.
Time to Recharge: Benefit from 32 paid vacation days (5 weeks + 5 extra) and paid parental leave to focus on what matters most.
Career Growth: Access professional development opportunities and grow your career in a high-impact, high-growth industry.
Stay Energized: Enjoy excellent coffee, tea, free snacks, and subsidized lunches at the office.
Stay Active: Use our Spirii gym and shared company car to keep moving.
Make an Impact: Be part of a team driving the future of sustainable energy while working on meaningful projects.
Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.


The position is a permanent position in Copenhagen, Denmark. Danish language is not a requirement, but fluent English language skills are required.

About Spirii – Who We Are

Spirii is a full-service e-Mobility provider, which offers hardware and software charging solutions to companies in the private and the public sector. Spirii has recently celebrated its six-year anniversary as a founded company; however the people in Spirii have extensive experience working with electric cars and charging, as well as the development of future transport and energy systems.

Spirii is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences. Spirii strives to create an inclusive and diverse workplace, and we encourage all qualified applicants to apply regardless of age, gender, race, sexual/religious orientation, or disability.

Title: Trading Analyst
Description:
Join us, be part of more.

We’re so much more than an energy company. We’re a family of brands revolutionising how we power the planet. We're energisers. One team of 21,000 colleagues that’s energising a greener, fairer future by creating an energy system that doesn’t rely on fossil fuels, whilst living our powerful commitment to igniting positive change in our communities. Here, you can find more purpose, more passion and more potential. That’s why working here is #MoreThanACareer. We do energy differently – we do it all. We make it, store it, move it, sell it and mend it.

About your team:

At Centrica Energy, our mission is to move it.

We’re energy movers by nature. We’re a global renewable energy trading company which helps move energy from source to use – powering businesses, homes, and societies as they transition to a new sustainable energy future. If the idea of working to create a sustainable energy future also moves you, we may very well be the right place for you.

A rare opportunity to shape trading decisions in a business operating at the intersection of analytics, optimisation and energy markets. This role brings together quantitative thinking, model development and close collaboration with traders and portfolio managers to improve performance, uncover market opportunities and support the optimisation of renewables, batteries and thermal assets across Europe, the UK and Ireland.

The team 

Trading Analytics is part of the Short-Term Asset Trading team, responsible for trading our renewables, battery portfolio and thermal plants across continental Europe and UK & Ireland. 

We maximise the value of Centrica Energy’s asset portfolio by: 


Delivering market, portfolio and performance insights. 
Improving trading strategies. 
Increasing automation. 
Driving the integration of algorithmic trading. 


The team plays a central role in the organisation and works closely with the trading team, algo development teams, portfolio management and commercial teams. This role is particularly well suited for people with a broad interest in our activities and commercial mindset.

About The Role

This role sits close to the commercial heartbeat of the business, providing analysis that helps translate complex market and operational dynamics into clear decision support. The Trading Analyst will work across data, modelling and trading activity to strengthen performance understanding and support better optimisation outcomes. It is a role for someone who enjoys combining quantitative rigour with commercial relevance in a fast-moving energy environment.


Support the optimisation and trading of a portfolio spanning renewable, battery and thermal assets across continental Europe and the UK & Ireland.
Deliver analysis and decision support to traders and portfolio managers, helping explain performance, identify market opportunities and assess risk.
Develop analytical models and tools that improve trading strategies, asset optimisation and forecasting capabilities.
Explore market and operational data to uncover value drivers, explain outcomes and identify new opportunities for value creation.
Contribute to the continued expansion of automation and algorithmic trading within the Short-Term Asset Trading team.


About You

The ideal candidate combines a structured, analytical mindset with the ability to work hands-on with data and quantitative models. A strong foundation in statistics, optimisation and programming is complemented by a curiosity for understanding energy markets and trading dynamics. Success in the role depends on the ability to translate complex problems into actionable insights while collaborating effectively across teams.

We are looking for someone who has:


A few years of relevant experience from a similar role.
Strong analytical capabilities, with a proven ability to explore data in depth and explain underlying drivers of performance.
Experience translating real-world challenges into mathematical optimisation problems and evaluating solution quality.
Solid understanding of probability, statistical distributions, and experience with back-testing and performance validation of algorithms.
Proficiency in Python development, including practical experience leveraging modern AI-assisted coding tools.
Experience in, or a clear interest in, power markets, renewables or energy systems, combined with strong communication skills and a collaborative mindset.


About Centrica Energy

Centrica Energy is a global energy trading and optimisation business that helps move energy from source to use, connecting producers, suppliers and offtakers across wholesale energy markets. The business trades power, gas, liquefied natural gas and green certificates, and operates from eight offices across three continents with the purpose of energising a greener, fairer future.

The business is one of the largest renewable asset managers in Europe, with more than 18.8 GW of capacity under contract, of which more than 85% is renewable assets. Alongside trading activity, Centrica Energy also provides optimisation and flexibility services across batteries, renewable flexibility, combined heat and power plants and other flexible assets, using advanced algorithms, market expertise and 24/7 operations to maximise value across major power markets.

Power trading activity spans major exchanges and OTC markets across Europe and the United States, covering intraday, day-ahead and longer-dated products, supported by advanced algorithmic trading capabilities. As the trading and optimisation arm of Centrica plc, Centrica Energy plays an important role in managing commodity risk, supporting the green transition and helping create more resilient and efficient energy systems.

A rare opportunity to shape trading decisions in a business operating at the intersection of analytics, optimisation and energy markets. This role brings together quantitative thinking, model development and close collaboration with traders and portfolio managers to improve performance, uncover market opportunities and support the optimisation of renewables, batteries and thermal assets across Europe, the UK and Ireland.

The team 

Trading Analytics is part of the Short-Term Asset Trading team, responsible for trading our renewables, battery portfolio and thermal plants across continental Europe and UK & Ireland. 

We maximise the value of Centrica Energy’s asset portfolio by: 

  • Delivering market, portfolio and performance insights. 
  • Improving trading strategies. 
  • Increasing automation. 
  • Driving the integration of algorithmic trading. 

The team plays a central role in the organisation and works closely with the trading team, algo development teams, portfolio management and commercial teams. This role is particularly well suited for people with a broad interest in our activities and commercial mindset.

About The Role

This role sits close to the commercial heartbeat of the business, providing analysis that helps translate complex market and operational dynamics into clear decision support. The Trading Analyst will work across data, modelling and trading activity to strengthen performance understanding and support better optimisation outcomes. It is a role for someone who enjoys combining quantitative rigour with commercial relevance in a fast-moving energy environment.

  • Support the optimisation and trading of a portfolio spanning renewable, battery and thermal assets across continental Europe and the UK & Ireland.
  • Deliver analysis and decision support to traders and portfolio managers, helping explain performance, identify market opportunities and assess risk.

Success in the role depends on the ability to translate complex problems into actionable insights while collaborating effectively across teams.

We are looking for someone who has:

  • A few years of relevant experience from a similar role.
  • Strong analytical capabilities, with a proven ability to explore data in depth and explain underlying drivers of performance.
  • Experience translating real-world challenges into mathematical optimisation problems and evaluating solution quality.
  • Solid understanding of probability, statistical distributions, and experience with back-testing and performance validation of algorithms.
  • Proficiency in Python development, including practical experience leveraging modern AI-assisted coding tools.

Title: Data Science & BI Developer - 12 month maternity cover
Place: Hørsholm, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Join our Golf Insights team and work hands-on with one of the world’s richest data ecosystems in golf. You’ll be appointed to specific product segments and deliver insights that foster data-driven decisions internally and external customers.

Our team is responsible for the transformational data modelling utilized in different consumption layers, driving meaningful accessible insights. We are on a continuous journey to strengthen our foundation of augmented analytics, which you will be expected to contribute to.

We collaborate closely with the product teams as well as various R&D functions, to deliver insights that drive product development and customer business success.

Your Profile

We Are Looking For a Data Science & BI Developer To Join Our Team On a 12-month Maternity Cover Contract. We Expect You To Have 3+ Years Of Experience In BI And Analytics Or Related Fields. Golf Knowledge And Passion For The Sport Is a Plus, While We Also Expect That You Are


Motivated, proactive, and a fast learner 
Curious about working with complex data 
Enthusiastic about using AI tools to work smarter and build better
Excited by the idea of building high-quality BI tools that have a real-world impact 
Eager to grow in an international, collaborative, and sports-driven environment 


You have some hands-on experience with SQL and dashboards (Power BI or similar), and ideally some exposure to Microsoft Fabric. You’re comfortable working with data from initial transformations to final reports, and you're energized by the opportunity to bring AI into how insights are created and consumed. 

Responsibilities


Build and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports 
Leverage AI development tools to accelerate BI deliverables and improve output quality
Explore and implement augmented analytics capabilities to enhance how end users interact with data
Develop and optimize data models and schemas 
Collaborate with data engineers to acquire and prepare data 
Work with product owners and internal stakeholders to define BI needs 


Job Requirements


A master's degree in Business Intelligence, Computer Science, Data Analytics or a similar data-related field
Experience writing SQL and working with relational databases 
Proficiency with Power BI or other dashboarding tools, while broader experience in Microsoft Fabric is a plus
Experience with dbt
A proactive, independent approach – you enjoy owning your tasks and learning as you go 
Fluent in English and comfortable working in a cross-cultural team 


The Office

We are located at DTU Science Park in Hørsholm. We take great pride in the state-of-art building we have designed! The office features bright meeting rooms, coffee areas to catch up with colleagues, a large testing arena with simulators available for employees and a fitness center, everything surrounded by a beautiful forest and respecting the environment. We made sure to utilize the newest technologies to be energy efficient and be mindful of ventilation and sound propagation. The cherry on top is our canteen: with an indoor and outdoor area to enjoy the fresh food prepared by our own kitchen staff.

Join the home of a powerful sports brand and a one-of-a-kind technology

Our proprietary technology is based on expert knowledge about radar, computer vision, data, and software engineering. Our solutions are developed by specialists who endlessly explore and challenge new technical boundaries. Trackman’s blend of cutting-edge technology, sports, and continued growth make our company an outstanding place to work.

Our work culture is entrepreneurial, ambitious, and rewarding as you get to collaborate with inspiring colleagues and interact with the leaders of golf, baseball, and football. Based on a unique brand reputation, we are proud to work in close partnership with the top athletes, organizations, and teams that rely on our technology to stay ahead of the race.

We are a Danish company founded, owned, and managed since 2003 by three entrepreneurs with a strong passion for sports. Today, we have a global footprint with our headquarters located in Denmark (Hørsholm) and offices in New York, Phoenix, Seoul and Tokyo. We employ over 1000+ people worldwide, half of which work in Denmark, where most hardware and software products are developed, tested, and manufactured.

Where innovation happens

At Trackman, we know that great people make great products. We believe that teams thrive, and innovation sparks in an atmosphere where people are encouraged to think for themselves, where everybody’s voices are heard, and where the best ideas prevail in the pursuit of exceptional solutions. This mix makes Trackman an inspiring company – for our customers and colleagues.

Share our Passion!

Applications are continuously assessed, so please send your application as soon as possible. For additional information about the position, please contact hiring manager; Mikkel Hansen at mha@trackman.com .

Title: Junior Quantitative Analyst
Description:
About Odigo Energy

Odigo Energy is a fast-growing energy trading company headquartered in Sydhavnen, Aarhus C. Our team of traders, analysts, and software developers is building a state-of-the-art trading infrastructure to drive innovation in the energy markets. With 13 full-time employees, we operate in a highly collaborative environment where quantitative research, technology, and trading come together to create measurable impact.

We are now looking for an entry-level / junior quantitative analyst who has recently graduated or is about to complete their studies this summer and wants to begin their career in algorithmic energy trading.




Role Overview

We are seeking a curious and driven graduate who is excited to apply mathematics, statistics, and programming skills to real-world financial markets. You will join a team that values initiative, independent thinking, and technical rigor. We provide deep domain knowledge in energy markets. You bring strong quantitative foundations and a willingness to learn fast.

This is a full-time position designed for someone at the beginning of their career who wants steep learning, real responsibility, and direct exposure to live trading environments.




Responsibilities

Develop and improve algorithmic trading strategies and forecasting models using real-time and historical data
Contribute to the design and expansion of internal trading and forecasting frameworks in Python
Analyze large datasets to identify market inefficiencies and trading opportunities
Collaborate closely with traders and developers to translate research into production systems
Continuously monitor and evaluate model performance in live markets




Qualifications

Recently completed or about to complete a degree in Mathematics, Physics, Economics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related quantitative field
Strong programming skills in Python. Experience with version control and structured software development practices is highly valued
Solid foundation in statistics, probability theory, and numerical methods
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities
Ability to work independently while contributing effectively to a small, high-performance team
Interest in financial markets and algorithmic trading




What We Offer

A full-time entry-level role in a fast-growing energy trading company
Direct exposure to live trading strategies and decision-making
Significant responsibility from day one
Rapid professional development in quantitative trading and energy markets
A collaborative environment where technical excellence and initiative are rewarded
The opportunity to grow with the company as it scales




If you are motivated to start your career in quantitative trading and want to work in a highly technical, impact-driven environment, we would love to hear from you.

Title: Data Insights Specialist
Description:
Vil du arbejde i en rolle, hvor du både skal forstå teknikken og kunne omsætte den til løsninger, der skaber værdi for kunden?




Din rolle

Rollen er til dig, der ikke nødvendigvis skal bygge alt selv, men som har nok praktisk erfaring til at forstå muligheder, begrænsninger og de tekniske valg, der skal træffes. Du skal kunne være en troværdig sparringspartner for både kunder og kolleger og være med til at sætte retning for, hvordan data bruges bedst.




Du kommer til at arbejde med moderne dataløsninger og være med til at forme løsninger med afsæt i især Google Cloud, Azure og Microsoft Fabric. Rollen kombinerer teknisk forståelse, løsningsdesign og kundekontakt. Det betyder, at du både skal kunne bidrage til konkrete løsninger og samtidig have blik for det større billede, kundens behov, datagrundlag, arkitektur og retning.




Du bliver en vigtig sparringspartner for kunderne og en del af den dialog, hvor behov omsættes til praktiske og teknisk holdbare løsninger. Samtidig kommer du til at arbejde tæt sammen med kolleger, der bygger og videreudvikler de tekniske løsninger i praksis.




Du kommer blandt andet til at:

bidrage til design og videreudvikling af dataplatforme, dataflows og cloud-baseret datainfrastruktur
arbejde med løsninger inden for SQL, Python og data-pipelines
omsætte forretningsbehov til tekniske løsninger i samarbejde med kunder og kolleger
deltage i kundedialog og rådgive om datagrundlag, muligheder og næste skridt
være med til at sætte retning for kunders arbejde med data

Din profil

Vi leder efter dig, der:

har et par års erfaring fra en rolle som fx Data Consultant, Analytics Engineer, BI Consultant, Data Engineer eller lignende
har praktisk erfaring med SQL og Python og forstår, hvordan moderne dataløsninger bygges
har forståelse for cloud, datainfrastruktur og moderne dataplatforme
forstår principperne bag datamodellering og data warehouse-løsninger
trives i dialogen med kunder og kan gøre tekniske problemstillinger forståelige
har lyst til at arbejde i spændet mellem teknik, rådgivning og forretning




Det er en fordel, hvis du har erfaring med Google Cloud, Azure, MS Fabric, Git eller data inden for marketing, web eller e-commerce.


Hvad du får

Hos Web2Media får du en rolle med både teknisk tyngde, kundekontakt og indflydelse. Du bliver en del af et fagligt stærkt miljø, hvor du er med til at forme løsninger, skabe retning og omsætte data til reel værdi for kunderne.




Vi vægter fællesskabet højt og gør en særlig indsats for at arrangere aktiviteter, der har fokus på trivsel, sundhed og arbejdsglæde. Herudover kan du se frem til:

Fleksible arbejdstider og mulighed for hjemmearbejdsdage
Fri fitness og rabatordninger
Frokostordning, god kaffe og lækker frugt
En fredagsbar samt årlige arrangementer
Sundhedsforsikring og pensionsordning
5 feriefridage efter 9 måneders anciennitet

Om Web2Media

Vi er et rådgivningshus med hovedkontor i hjertet af Aarhus og tæller pt. ca., 80 ildsjæle. Kulturen er åben og ligefrem, og vi vil altid hinanden, vores kunder og leverandører det bedste. Vi sætter vores ambitioner højt, men er samtidigt nede på jorden som mennesker og vægter ordentlighed, integritet og kvalitet.

Læs mere om vores virksomhedskultur her.

 

 

Praktisk

Lyder ovenstående som dig, så send din ansøgning og dit CV til os. Du giver med din ansøgning tilladelse til, at dine data evt. deles med ekstern samarbejdspartner inden for rekruttering. Data behandles i henhold til GDPR.

Arbejdssted: Aarhus C

Opstart: Snarest muligt

Ansøgning: Samtaler afholdes løbende

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Jonas Nørgaard Nielsen på mail: jnn@Web2Media.dk.

Title: Dataanalytisk udvikler / BI-udvikler til Travelmind Group
Description:
Vil du være med til at gøre data til en aktiv del af forretningen – ikke kun i rapporter, men direkte i de systemer, beslutninger og processer, vi arbejder med hver dag?




Travelmind Group søger en dataanalytisk udvikler, der kan hjælpe os med at udnytte de store mængder data, vi allerede har på tværs af vores rejse- og feriebrands.




Vi driver blandt andet Campaya Sommerhusudlejning, skiferie.dk, Elcano Travel, Caddie Golfrejser og flere andre rejsebrands på fælles, egenudviklede platforme. Forretningen er i kraftig vækst, og vi arbejder målrettet med at blive endnu bedre til at bruge data til at skabe bedre kundeoplevelser, højere konvertering, bedre prissætning og mere effektive interne arbejdsgange.

Vi søger derfor en profil, der både kan forstå data, forretning og udvikling – og som kan være med til at gøre data langt mere tilgængelig og anvendelig i hele organisationen.




Rollen

Du bliver en del af vores udviklingsteam og kommer til at arbejde tæt sammen med både ledelse, salg, marketing, kundeservice og produktudvikling.




Din opgave bliver at hjælpe os med at gøre data mere anvendelig i hele forretningen. Ikke kun som rapporter og dashboards, men også som en integreret del af vores egne systemer, hvor data kan bruges til at skabe bedre beslutninger, bedre kundeoplevelser og mere intelligente digitale løsninger.




Vi har allerede store mængder data om blandt andet bookinger, priser, søgninger, produkter, kunder, husejere, salgskanaler og performance. Vi ønsker at blive langt bedre til at omsætte disse data til konkrete indsigter, automatiseringer og værktøjer, der kan bruges i hverdagen.




Det kan eksempelvis være data, der hjælper os med at forstå, hvorfor nogle ferieboliger performer bedre end andre, hvordan priser kan optimeres, hvordan søgninger og anbefalinger kan forbedres, eller hvor der er potentiale for vækst og effektivisering.




Dine opgaver kan blandt andet blive

Opbygning af rapporter og dashboards til ledelse, salg, marketing og drift
Udvikling af datagrundlag, datamodeller og integrationer på tværs af vores platforme
Analyse af bookingdata, konvertering, priser, belægning og kundeadfærd
Udvikling af modeller og værktøjer til prissætningsforslag, anbefalinger og optimering
Forbedring af søgning, sortering og produktvisning baseret på data
Segmentering af kunder, husejere og produkter
Automatisering af manuelle analyser og interne processer
Samarbejde med udviklere om at integrere dataindsigter direkte i vores platform
Eksperimenter med machine learning og AI-modeller, hvor det kan give reel forretningsmæssig værdi




Vi forestiller os, at du




Har en stærk analytisk tilgang og trives med at kombinere teknik, data og forretning.




Du behøver ikke passe præcist ind i én bestemt titel. Din baggrund kan eksempelvis være som BI-udvikler, data engineer, analytisk softwareudvikler, data analyst med stærke tekniske kompetencer eller lignende.




Det er en fordel, hvis du har erfaring med flere af følgende områder:

SQL og relationelle databaser, gerne SQL Server
Datamodellering, rapportering og BI
Power BI, Data Studio eller lignende værktøjer
Python eller R til analyse, databehandling eller modellering
C# og .NET-udvikling
Arbejde med API’er og integrationer
Analyse af kommercielle data, fx salg, priser, konvertering eller kundeadfærd
Machine learning, AI-modeller eller predictive analytics
At omsætte forretningsbehov til konkrete tekniske løsninger




Det vigtigste er, at du kan skabe bro mellem data og praktisk anvendelse. Vi leder efter en person, der både kan dykke ned i komplekse datasæt og samtidig forstå, hvordan resultaterne kan bruges i virkeligheden.




Om os




Travelmind Group er en dansk rejsekoncern med en række specialiserede rejsebrands, der alle bygger på fælles, egenudviklede teknologiske platforme.




Vores systemer håndterer store mængder produkter, priser, tilgængelighed, kunder, bookinger og marketingdata. Vi arbejder med online salg, automatisering, API-integrationer, SEO, performance marketing og løbende optimering af vores digitale platforme.




Vi har kontorer i blandt andet Horsens, Glostrup, Stockholm og Oslo.




Vi tilbyder

En central rolle i udviklingen af vores datadrevne arbejde
Mulighed for at påvirke både strategi, systemer og konkrete forretningsbeslutninger
Et udviklingsmiljø med korte beslutningsveje og stor praktisk indflydelse
Arbejde med data, der har direkte kommerciel betydning
Mulighed for at kombinere BI, softwareudvikling, data engineering og AI
Et uformelt miljø med dygtige kolleger og højt tempo
En virksomhed i vækst, hvor der er mange uløste og spændende dataopgaver




Hvem er du?




Du er nysgerrig, analytisk og teknisk stærk. Du motiveres af at finde mønstre, bygge løsninger og gøre komplekse data forståelige og anvendelige.




Du behøver ikke være specialist i alt fra dag ét, men du skal have lyst til at arbejde i krydsfeltet mellem softwareudvikling, dataanalyse, BI og forretning.




Du trives med at tage ansvar, stille spørgsmål og finde frem til de løsninger, der faktisk skaber værdi – ikke kun teknisk, men også kommercielt.




Ansøgning

Lyder det som en spændende udfordring, hører vi meget gerne fra dig.

Send din ansøgning og CV til:

cp@travelmind.group

Title: Data Science - Manager - Retail
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

As a Manager for Data Science consulting, you operate at the intersection of business strategy, data, and emerging AI technologies. You work closely with clients to shape their data and AI agendas—translating business challenges into actionable opportunities, advising on the right approaches, and guiding initiatives from idea through to value realization.

You bring a strong technical foundation, but your primary focus is on understanding client needs, framing problems, and designing impactful solutions. You are a trusted advisor who can connect strategy, data, technology, and organizational change—while staying hands-on enough to explore data, prototype solutions, and leverage modern AI tools.

Qualifications


Strong experience in data, AI, or analytics roles, with exposure to both technical implementation and business-facing work
Proven ability to engage clients, structure problems, and translate business needs into data/AI solutions
Proven experience in developing and deploying AI models at scale, including natural language processing (NLP), computer vision, and reinforcement learning.
Expertise in machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and scikit-learn, as well as experience with cloud-based AI platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure).
Strong understanding of data engineering concepts, including data pipelines and big data technologies such as Spark, Hadoop, SQL, and NoSQL.
Experience working with modern AI ecosystems (e.g., cloud platforms, LLMs, AI tools, or data platforms)
Strong communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at multiple levels
Comfortable working in ambiguous environments, exploring new problem spaces, and expanding into adjacent domains
Demonstrated curiosity and eagerness to learn and apply new technologies, especially within the rapidly evolving AI landscape
Experience in consulting, digital transformation, or cross-functional environments is a strong plus


Additional Information

Set Yourself Apart With


Experience defining AI or data strategies for organizations
Hands-on experience with modern AI tools (e.g., generative AI, copilots, automation platforms)
Exposure to data architecture, data engineering, or product development
Track record of driving AI adoption or innovation initiatives within organizations
Advanced degree in a relevant field (e.g., data science, engineering, business, or similar)


What We’re Looking For

We’re looking for someone who is:


Equally comfortable in the boardroom and with a dataset
A translator between business ambition and technical reality
Curious, adaptable, and excited about the fast-moving AI landscape
Motivated to go beyond pure data science into broader problem-solving and innovation


Company Description

Publicis Sapient is a digital transformation partner helping established organizations get to their future, digitally enabled state, both in the way they work and the way they serve their customers. We help unlock value through a start-up mindset and modern methods, fusing strategy, consulting, and customer experience with agile engineering and problem-solving creativity. United by our core values and our purpose of helping people thrive in the brave pursuit of the next, our 20,000+ people in 53 offices around the world combine experience across technology, data sciences, consulting, and customer obsession to accelerate our clients’ businesses through designing the products and services their customers truly value.

Title: Analytics Engineer for Analytics in PeB Denmark
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job ID: 1326

Analytics Engineer for Analytics in Personal Banking (PeB) Denmark

We are growing our team in PeB Analytics in Denmark. We are looking for a person that loves working with data and analysis to join our existing Analytics team. In PeB we are a group of specialists in various fields: everything from digital marketing and content production to web analysis and data science. Collectively, we are responsible for engaging new and existing personal customers in digital and offline channels, and driving sales and engagement.

Every day, we work hard to ensure that each individual customer or employee in Nordea receives personalized follow-up and advice. We relevant at the right time in a personalised approach. 

Joining us means you’ll have an impact on how we do banking – today and tomorrow. So bring your ideas, skills and unique background. With us, you’ll be in good company with plenty of opportunities to collaborate, grow and make your mark on something bigger.

About This Opportunity  

This is a role within Personal Banking Analytics Denmark / Personal Banking Denmark. In Personal Banking we are building the omnichannel customer journey seamless experience and for that we need to connect different data sources across our platforms and applications. We want to send relevant personalised messages to our customers offering the right product or service, in the right channel, at the right time.

We are looking for a curious and driven Analytics Engineer that is responsible for development and maintenance of local data storage to enable data availability and efficient utilization possibilities for local analytics team members. You will be providing clean, accurate and high quality datasets. These tasks are crucial part of implementing our Data and Analytics Strategy. You will collaborate with pears from a Nordic point of view to drive these initiatives from a Nordic Scale point of view. Note that this is more a business role than an IT role.

Joining the team will provide you with opportunities to use your experience with data management, data analysis, data engineering and business analysis, to support data-savvy end-users. 

What You Will Be Doing

As the Analytics Engineer, you will empower end users to answer their own questions regarding for instance product portfolio analysis, sales funnels, campaign performance and customer segmentation. You will be working on setting up clean data sets and data modelling/structure that allows us to have the same view across different teams. This means making use of the infrastructure available in Nordea to write data transformation jobs through code and create reusable data assets for analysts and data scientists that get updated in an automated way with a given frequency.

In addition you will be:


Extracting the underlying data from existing databases and centralizing it to a local data storage 
Driving Data Strategy with Nordic initiatives 
Writing (complex) data transformations in SQL, using the available data building tools in Nordea, that will create the metrics needed for the analysts
Documenting the process for building the different data sets to ensure a high quality foundation for data analysist/scientists. Maintaining data documentation and definitions
Collaborating very close with Data Scientists and Business requestors within PeB Denmark and Nordic counterparties
Participating actively in development initiatives within Digital Sales teams to expand the usage of data in marketing campaigns
Supporting the maintenance or development of reports and analysis tools (e.g. PowerBI, Airflow and DBT) 


Who You Are

You are a person who understands data. You can analyse it to answer queries, but also understand where potential limitations exist and can creatively resolve them in either a tactical or strategic manner. You have a blended profile of skills and mindset of a data engineer, data scientist and a data analyst. You take ownership of your tasks, and enjoy data gathering, cleaning, engineering, and documenting as you build.

You have good interpersonal and communication skills to ask the right questions to stakeholders and colleagues. You are comfortable working on your own but also as part of a small team. You thrive in a cross-functional set up.

To build a successful career with us, you’ll work well with others and always act with customer’s needs in mind. You love learning and trying new things, and you’re excited about bringing your ideas to the table. You’re honest and reliable. You’re passionate about doing a great job.

To succeed in this role, we believe that you are/have:


Knowledge of software/data engineering best practices
Can sketch a data model/data structure and implement it
Able to clearly communicate complex issues in an easy and understandable manner to a wide range of stakeholders
A team player - work closely with other specialists, involve stakeholders always asking questions and investigating alternative solutions
Innovative, openminded and excited about bringing your ideas to the table. You strive for continuous improvement and love learning and trying out new things
Dependable, willing to speak up even when it’s difficult, and committed to empowering others


Your experience and background:


Academic degree in highly quantitative discipline or specialisation such as Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, Software Engineering
2-3+ years of experience working in data-driven landscapes. Ideally you have experience as a data engineer and have discovered the business aspect, or you have been working as a data analyst and have strong tech experience
Strong SQL skills. Knowledge of Bitbucket, Dagster, dbt, Bamboo. Our main platform is Snowflake
Proficient in written and spoken English
To be able to write and speak Danish is an advantage


Your application will benefit from demonstrating experience in the below:


You know your ways around tools like Snowflake, Airflow, DBT, Git and Power BI
Skills in web frameworks such as Angular
Experience in data analysis, e.g. Python & R
You understand the principles of working with an Agile development approach


The role is based in Copenhagen, Denmark.

If this sounds like you, get in touch!

Next steps

Submit your application no later than 24/06/2026. For more information, you’re welcome to contact Martin Zinglersen at martin.zinglersen@nordea.com.

We enable dreams and aspirations for a greater good.

We build relationships. We add a personal touch to everything we do – when advising our customers, collaborating with colleagues, and meeting our potential candidates.

We learn and develop. We take pride in being experts and thinking ahead. We use our expertise to meet our customers’ needs, from the simplest to the most complex. We bring a growth mindset to our work that enables us to focus on a broader perspective in our daily challenges.

We lead change. We are responsible and aware of the impact of our decisions, both for our customers and for our local and global communities. Mindful of our responsibility towards current and future generations, we have made sustainability an integrated part of our business strategy.

We are Nordea. We have a 200-year history of supporting and growing the Nordic economies and our values are deeply rooted in these open, progressive and collaborative societies. As one of the biggest employers in the Nordics, Poland and Estonia, you have excellent opportunities to evolve, develop and move forward with us.
